meanings.mnemonic: You'll work your soul to death if you're too busy . Being busy just isn't good for your soul. Take breaks. Go on vacation. Don't be so busy that you destroy yourself.
meanings.hint: Look around you. See all those busy people? Their souls are dying. Don't end up like them.

readings.mnemonic: Take time out of your busy life and go bow ling ( ぼう ) once in a while. In fact, you should probably go bowling once a week at least. Nothing brings energy back to your life like some relaxing bowling.
readings.hint: Think about your busy schedule this week. Now cancel everything and just go bowling instead.

meanings.mnemonic: Passed out in front of your gate lies a former leader . He's now a drunkard , but once ran a popular clique called the Cool Kids Clan . The clan has fallen apart, and he no longer has a clique to call his own.
meanings.hint: Imagine this washed up drunkard leader passed out in front of the gate to your house. You have to pass him whenever you enter or exit your house. He's gross and he smells. Experience the stench in your nostrils. How he was ever the leader of a clan you have no idea.

readings.mnemonic: Eventually you get sick and tired of this washed up clan leader and you gather your own clique to chase this guy away from your gate and your property. You and your friends grab some bats ( ばつ ) and use these bats to beat the drunkard leader. Being beaten with bats frightens him, and he scurries away.
readings.hint: Hear the crushing blows of bats against flesh as you imagine this scene in your mind. You're beating the crap out of this guy with a bunch of bats, but hey — he had it coming.

meanings.mnemonic: To a narwhal , a towel is just normal cloth . They're always wet so towel cloth is all they know.
meanings.hint: Picture a narwhal wrapped in a fuzzy towel blanket, with a towel tail cover, and some towel underpants. Everything is made of this type of cloth.

readings.mnemonic: If you use this type of cloth for your clothes and blankets people will call you a foo l ( ふ ). They think you're a fool who can't tell the difference between human cloth and towel cloth.
readings.hint: "You fool, you aren't a narwhal, that cloth isn't for you!" they say. They're so mean.

meanings.mnemonic: At the edge of the cliff , two tree s mark the spot where you must stop , since any continuation requires a way across. The trees seem long enough to reach to the other side, but how are you going to chop them down?
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself on this cliff. You desire a continuation for your journey, not wanting to stop here. Feel the strong wind on your body, blowing your hair about as you stand on the edge of the cliff. The leaves on the trees rustle in the breeze.

readings.mnemonic: Continuation is impossible unless you chop these trees down to make a bridge to the other side. You decide to use a wrecki ng ( れき ) ball. Luckily, there's one right by you. So you take the wrecking ball and knock the trees over, making a bridge with them.
readings.hint: Picture yourself using the wrecking ball to knock down the two trees on the cliff. Hear the the trees crack and splinter from the force of the wrecking ball. That wrecking ball is the sound of continuation.

meanings.mnemonic: You have to gather thread to sell if you want to continue living. You want to make a living, don't you? Make sure you continue to sell that thread every single day!
meanings.hint: Imagine selling thread so you can continue living. You search for thread continually now. But are you gathering thread to continue to live, or living to continue gathering thread?

readings.mnemonic: You have to continue to sell that thread because a Boso zoku ( ぞく ) bike gang is forcing you to do it. You see? If you want to continue to live, you have to appease this Bosozoku gang. You better keep selling!
readings.hint: Continue selling thread and paying off the Bosozoku. It's the only way you can continue to survive!

meanings.mnemonic: Direct a tsunami into a canine 's mouth to decrease the amount of water that strikes the surrounding area.
meanings.hint: Just make sure you get a really thirsty canine, because they're going to need to drink a LOT of water to decrease the tsunami's power.

readings.mnemonic: After you and your canine decrease the tsunami's power, Gen ji ( げん ) comes out to thank you. That tsunami was on track to wash away Genji's house, which would have forced him to majorly decrease his frivolous playboy activities.
readings.hint: Now you're wondering if you made the right choice. The world might actually be a better place if Genji decreased his silly lover-boy antics a bit.

meanings.mnemonic: The easy radical and the easy kanji are the same!
meanings.hint: Easy is easy!

readings.mnemonic: What would be an easy reading for this kanji? い ! The sound easy starts with!
readings.hint: I told you easy was easy.

meanings.mnemonic: If you put a thread in the middle of a kiss , you’re going to entangle it. Things can get pretty messy in a kiss, so adding a thread to the mix will definitely entangle it.
meanings.hint: Imagine trying to kiss someone with a piece of thread between your lips. Feel the thread getting all entangled between the two of you. Thread entangled, tongues entangled, lips entangled — just a thready kiss of entanglement.

readings.mnemonic: This isn't going well, so you pull out the weird kiss thread and entangle it around a rack ( らく ). Good thing that rack was there! Much better to have it entangled around a rack when you're trying to kiss someone.
readings.hint: Imagine pausing this kiss to pull a thread out of your mouth and entangle it around a random rack in your room. Smooth move, Casanova! Your partner will totally love that.

meanings.mnemonic: A flag loiter s in a black hole . This flag marks (in the black hole) where you left your footwear .
meanings.hint: If you look into the black hole, you can see your footwear right there, marked with the flag.

readings.mnemonic: You grab your footwear from the black hole. In your footwear (thanks to the shelter they provided), some ree ds ( り ) have grown in!
readings.hint: It's amazing that these cute little reeds could grow in your footwear in the middle of a black hole. As a great philosopher once said, life finds a way.

meanings.mnemonic: If you put a roof over a valley it will change its form . Its appearance will alter completely!
meanings.hint: Picture a valley in your mind. Now put a roof on it. Hmm. It isn't shape d like a valley anymore.

readings.mnemonic: Its new appearance looks familiar to you, like it's a big yo gurt ( よう ) cup! A valley with a roof on it looks exactly like a yogurt cup. Huh!
readings.hint: Now imagine this taking shape before your eyes and becoming actual yogurt! You grab a giant spoon and take a bite.

meanings.mnemonic: Within the bamboo grove, someone asks your reason for being there, and you explain it's to harvest bamboo to craft a flute .
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself cutting down some bamboo, thinking, "This is the reason I'm here, I need a flute, ASAP!" There is no other reason to be here besides this.

readings.mnemonic: You cut down the bamboo and whittle a flute . You start playing a song. You know the reason why. An alien spacecraft comes flying down and drops a box full of alien tech ( てき ) in front of you.
readings.hint: You've done what you came to do. You summoned an alien craft with your bamboo flute, gaining some alien tech in the process. Open up the box to see what tech awaits you.

meanings.mnemonic: The history radical and the history kanji are the same.
meanings.hint: Know your radicals, yo!

readings.mnemonic: History was full of famous shee p ( し ). Sure there are people and stuff, but historical sheep are where it's at. There's the famous sheep samurai, the death sheep who works for the yakuza, just tons!
readings.hint: Look at a history book, behind each of those humans was a sheep, changing the world as we know it.

meanings.mnemonic: If the sun is compare d to an insect , you’ll see that they're both shiny, but that the sun is much shinier than the insect.
meanings.hint: Imagine holding up an insect to the sun to compare the two. See how they both shine, but the sun shines more brightly than the insect.

readings.mnemonic: While you’re holding up an insect to the sun for comparison, a flock of con dors ( こん ) flies by, blocking out the sun. There are so many condors that they manage to totally block out the sun.
readings.hint: Feel how dark and cold it is suddenly with all those condors blocking out the sun.

meanings.mnemonic: Use your mouth to take a measurement of this group . Just stretch your mouth out and around every single person. Get 'em all in there.
meanings.hint: I hope this group is full of people who enjoy being inside someone else's mouth, because they're gonna be pretty mad when you let them out if they aren't.

readings.mnemonic: This group is so big it takes you till dawn ( だん ) to get your measurement: they weigh a ton ( とん ) too!
readings.hint: Imagine letting this group of, now very wet, people out of your mouth one by one. They don't seem to care that they weigh a ton! That's a large measurement, they should be proud.

meanings.mnemonic: “Don’t loiter – run , Junior !” said Indiana Jones Senior to Indiana Jones Junior. They’re escaping from a sticky situation and Junior was just loitering around like a fool. It’s no time to loiter, it’s time to run!
meanings.hint: Imagine Sean Connery saying “Don’t loiter – run, Junior!” and you should have a much easier time remembering this phrase if you remember it in his voice.

readings.mnemonic: Deciding not to loiter any longer, Indiana Jones Junior takes his dad’s advice and starts to run. Unfortunately he wasn’t paying attention to where his feet were and he stubbed his to e ( と ) on a jagged rock. His toe is in so much pain that he can’t bear to run any longer.
readings.hint: Imagine that you are Indiana Jones Junior and this is happening to you. Feel the terrible pain of your throbbing toe as you clutch it, with the bad guys catching up with you.

meanings.mnemonic: You jumped off your roof for some reason and now you're floating in midair ! You kick your legs a bit and move your arms like you're in water, but you're still just stuck there in midair.
meanings.hint: At the moment you can't remember the reason you did it, but when you jumped off your roof you just floated in midair.

readings.mnemonic: As you spin a bit in midair you look up and see Chew bacca ( ちゅう ) looking down at you from a spaceship. He's laughing his Chewbacca laugh and you realize he has you trapped in his tractor beam.
readings.hint: Oh Chewbacca, you lovable fuzzball. You better not drop me in midair, or I'll die. I hope you bring me up there!

meanings.mnemonic: A raging tsunami rises next to the calm sun . When you try to compare these two things, it's clear that they cannot mix . If a tsunami is coming, it will likely get in the way of the sun.
meanings.hint: Imagine the sun getting blocked out as a tsunami rises up from the ocean. Feel the cold fear of death on you as the tsunami approaches. That tsunami is gonna mix you up.

readings.mnemonic: Just before the tsunami crashes over you, a con dor ( こん ) swoops out of the sky and grabs you up. This condor must be a mix — a normal condor is not strong enough to pick up a human. The condor must be mixed with something else. Like a pterodactyl.
readings.hint: Imagine yourself getting mixed up in this situation and the pterodactyl-sized condor picking you up. Feel the condor's talons digging into your shoulders as it grabs you up and flies you towards the sun.

meanings.mnemonic: A sheep loses its horns on the ground so it picks them up in its mouth to take them to someone who's morally good enough to attach them back onto its head. Sheep don’t have hands, so the sheep had to pick the horns up off the ground with his mouth. Someone evil would just murder the sheep and steal his horns, so the sheep must find someone good to reaffix them.
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself as the good person being approached by this forlorn sheep without horns. Hear the clack of the horns together as the sheep drops them out of his mouth onto the ground and lets out a pathetic bleat. “Baa~aa~ah~”

readings.mnemonic: Since you are morally good , you decide not to murder the sheep and harvest its organs. Instead, as a practitioner of the good teachings of Zen ( ぜん ) Buddhism, you decided to help the sheep out. Your Zen teachings have increased the power of your healing abilities, so you fix the sheep up in no time.
readings.hint: Imagine yourself using your Zen magic to heal this sheep. Place your hands on the sheep’s horns and imagine returning them to their rightful location on the sheep’s head. Feel the soft wool of the sheep as he bleats in happiness.

meanings.mnemonic: You use a roof to keep yourself dry and safe from outer space . Look outside, above your roof, and you'll see outer space stretching above you.
meanings.hint: You don't want to float up into outer space and get all wet, do you? No? Then you better make sure you have a roof above your head.

readings.mnemonic: Why isn't it dry in outer space , you ask? Because of all the u ni ( う ) swimming around up there. They have nasty, wet, pointy spikes and they're all over the place.
readings.hint: Look closely up at outer space and you'll see all of the uni floating around up there. They're really dark, so it can be hard to notice them, but I promise you, they're there.

meanings.mnemonic: When they migrate to a river , a group of geoducks must do so in a certain order and sequence .
meanings.hint: Geoducks usually live in the ocean, but when they migrate to a river, they do so in a specific, predetermined order. Imagine what that sequence might be. Maybe in order of neck length, with the longest-necked geoducks going first? Or maybe in alphabetical order?

readings.mnemonic: Actually, they go in birth-month order , starting from June ( じゅん ). No one knows why they follow this June-based sequence . But the geoducks born in June always go first in the migration order.
readings.hint: Imagine it's June, and you're on a beach somewhere watching a bunch of geoducks make their way to a river. Marvel at the biological clock that determines the June-based migration sequence for these fascinating creatures.

meanings.mnemonic: What you say to the director is judged immediately. You're having trouble understanding this part of speech , so he's trying to help you. But you say it over and over and he keeps saying you're wrong.
meanings.hint: "Is it an adjective?" "No, that's the wrong part of speech." "Is it a verb?" "N-no! Of course not!"

readings.mnemonic: The director asks you again, "What is the part of speech for the word shee p ( し )?"
readings.hint: You stare blankly at him. What part of speech is that?! It's an animal, you know that. Oh! A noun. Duh.

meanings.mnemonic: If you see some flowers that are dry , pull them up and you'll find a potato .
meanings.hint: Potatoes grow when flowers dry out. All the nutrients are going to the potato under the ground, so the flowers up top get sucked dry.

readings.mnemonic: Potato es are totally emo ( いも ) because of the way they dry out flowers. Emo people and potatoes both like to dry out flowers.
readings.hint: Picture an emo person sitting alone in a dark room, staring at a potato. "You and I are the same."

meanings.mnemonic: If you stab somebody in the tongue with an umbrella , it’s sure to start a riot and cause disorder . Nobody likes to get stabbed in the tongue with an umbrella, so they’re probably going to retaliate, and before you know it, you’ve a full scale riot on your hands. Look what you’ve done.
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself getting stabbed in the tongue with an umbrella. It’s very painful. Taste the blood and metal in your mouth as this happens to you.

readings.mnemonic: Just now you realize that the man you stabbed in the tongue with your umbrella was none other than Macho Man Ran dy Savage ( らん ). Being a professional wrestler, Macho Man Randy Savage is ready to throw down at a moment’s notice. A riot immediately erupts. It’s a battle royal with you and Randy at the epicenter.
readings.hint: Picture yourself squaring off with Macho Man Randy Savage in the middle of this riot. You have the advantage of having an umbrella, but Randy has the advantage of being himself. Picture yourself getting into a fight with him.

meanings.mnemonic: It's important to focus oneself in winter so that one can emerge renew ed! You can't really go outside in winter without getting cold or sick, so spend winter focusing on YOU.
meanings.hint: Imagine sitting in your house all winter, meditating and thinking about how you can renew yourself.

readings.mnemonic: Winter ends and, renew ed, you come out ready to start a new hobby: kay aking ( かい )! Nothing says renewal like a brand new kayak and sense of purpose in life.
readings.hint: Imagine being back in your house in winter. You're thinking about all the kayaking you're gonna do when winter ends. You're even making the movements with your arms, so you'll be a really renewed you.

meanings.mnemonic: When the sun gets together with water , only violence can ensue. They don’t mix well together. The sun loves to fight water and water loves to fight the sun. Lots and lots of violence.
meanings.hint: Picture this violent fight scene in your brain. Imagine water locked in battle with the sun as the sun’s fire flares up and the water starts to sizzle.

readings.mnemonic: But after a while, the sun and the water have had enough of violence . They decide to settle their differences with a friendly game of bow ling ( ぼう ). Bowling is a great way to relieve stress and in the end, they bond over it.
readings.hint: Imagine the sun and the water trying to bowl. Hear the sound of bowling balls rolling down the lane and pins rattling around and crashing together.

meanings.mnemonic: The tongue radical and the tongue kanji are exactly the same! Yee-haw!

readings.mnemonic: You’re sound asleep in bed when your tongue drops out of your mouth and you start to drool, getting saliva all over the bed sheet ( した ). You wake up with a soggy patch of sheet near your mouth and wipe the drool off your face. Your significant other sees the whole thing.
readings.hint: Your significant other had been watching you sleep with your tongue out for a while now, so you're super embarrassed you wet the sheets with your nasty tongue drool. You feel very judged and embarrassed.

meanings.mnemonic:
On the bottom of bamboo root s, there’s a stamp that tells you the season in which it was cut.

Additionally, the stamp also has information on the condition of the bamboo’s joint s, because no one wants to buy bamboo with damaged joints!
meanings.hint: Picture yourself in a store, looking at a bunch of different bamboo stamps to make sure you don’t buy one that’s out of season, or one that has damaged joints. The season and the joints, that’s what quality bamboo is all about, and it’s why we have these stamps!

readings.mnemonic: These season stamps are marked on bamboo sets ( せつ ). You can't buy a single banana or a single grape, and just like that, if you want some nice seasonal bamboo, you have to buy them in sets.
readings.hint: How many bamboo sets will you be buying this season? I'm getting 42 sets.

meanings.mnemonic: Give some shellfish to a talent ed person and they'll have no problem accumulating wealth . People with talent can make money out of pretty much anything, so shellfish are like easy-mode.
meanings.hint: Picture the talented person huddled over a pile of shellfish, whispering to them about the wealth they'll soon be turned into.

readings.mnemonic: This talented person really starts accumulating wealth when their cy borg ( さい ) is unveiled. It's a half-man, half-shellfish cyborg that plays the xy lophone  ( ざい ), and everyone wants to buy it.
readings.hint: You'd be wealthy too if you sold that cyborg you made. It may not have any shellfish in it and it can't play the xylophone, but you could change that…

meanings.mnemonic: Your scooter and your car is what you always take along with you. Make sure they're connect ed well so you don't lose them!
meanings.hint: Your scooter and your car, connected by a tiny little string, are the two things you take along with you wherever you go.

readings.mnemonic: You take along your scooter and your car. Anything else, you can do a ren tal ( れん ) for!
readings.hint: The roof over your head? Rental. The clothes on your body? Rental. All of your friends? Rental. The only thing you permanently take along with you are your scooter and your car.

meanings.mnemonic: In spirit valley , spirits are abundant . Hence the name. Spirit valley has over two billion spirits floating in it.
meanings.hint: Just picture those forest spirits from that famous anime film. The woods are full of them. An abundant valley of spirits.

readings.mnemonic: In spirit valley, it's not just spirits that are abundant . There is an abundance of you ths ( ゆう ) as well.
readings.hint: Imagine an abundant number of spirits and youths hanging out, drinking microbrews and eating avocado toast. Spirit Valley is a weird place.

meanings.mnemonic: There are flowers on your right to show that you're still young . Your body still works, you're happy, you have your entire life ahead of you! And everything smells like flowers.
meanings.hint: Look to your right, if you see flowers (metaphorically, maybe) then you're still young. Congratulations!

readings.mnemonic: When young people look to the right, they'll see their future: a walke r ( わか ). You know, those metal contraptions with tennis balls on the bottoms to make sure they don't scrape the floor. Everything may be flowers and happiness now, but your future is in a walker.
readings.hint: All young people have a walker looming just off to the side. A sign that your youth won't last forever, dear summer child.

meanings.mnemonic: You take a stone to the forehead of a turkey . It is certain that you will damage it.
meanings.hint: Try hitting a sponge on a turkey's forehead. Not certain. Now a stone. Certain .

readings.mnemonic: You're killing this turkey, and you need to make sure he's dead for certain . The reason you need to make sure this turkey is dead is because of the cock ( かく ) in your life. Male chickens get really jealous when turkeys are around.
readings.hint: As you smash the stone into the turkey's forehead, yell out "This is for you, my cock! THIS IS FOR YOU!" Your cock lover watches from the shadows.

meanings.mnemonic: You take some flag s, nine in total, and put them one by one up your butt .
meanings.hint: Imagine taking these nine flags... count 'em... and putting them up your butt. With each one that's up there it gets harder and harder to fit the next one in.

readings.mnemonic: Now that you have nine flags inside your butt , you have to get them out. But, you don't know how to. So you ask Siri ( しり ), "How do I get nine flags out of my butt?"
readings.hint: In your head, doing the Siri voice, come up with an answer for your nine-flags-in-the-butt predicament.

meanings.mnemonic: That outfit on your skin is there to cover you up. In polite society, it's expected that you cover your skin with some kind of outfit — even if you have to wear something that causes you to incur damage, like circulation-cutting skinny jeans or a shirt with a really scratchy tag.
meanings.hint: Imagine the most uncomfortable outfit you can. Your body is incurring some minor damage right now, but hey — at least you're covering your skin properly.

readings.mnemonic: Another reason to cover yourself is to retain he at ( ひ ). Actually, retaining heat and staying warm is probably the reason people started covering themselves up in the first place.
readings.hint: Even if you incur some damage from uncomfortable clothing, it's better to cover up and retain heat than to underdress and incur damage from frostbite or something.

meanings.mnemonic: Under a tree a drunkard sits with twenty little contraptions . There's little electronic ones, little bopping ones, little walking ones... twenty of them.
meanings.hint: Can you even imagine twenty completely unique contraptions? Why don't you try, just for fun.

readings.mnemonic: You want to know what these contraptions are for, so you ask. The drunkard says, "These are kay ak ( かい ) contraptions *urp* they're for your uh *blarp* kayak... things."
readings.hint: "Kayak contraptions" is pretty vague, but you do have a kayak, so you're tempted anyway. If only you could figure out what each contraption did so you could justify buying some.

meanings.mnemonic: In the animal kingdom, a snake is the most likely to commit a crime . Remember the Garden of Eden? The snake was the bad guy. Ever since then, snakes are the animal most known for committing crimes.
meanings.hint: Picture a snake as a hardened criminal. See this snake with many prison tattoos and muscles from working out in his jail cell. Hear the snake hissing as it slithers about to commit yet another crime.

readings.mnemonic: This snake has evaded the law for too long. It’s time for a true hero to put an end to its crime spree. Enter Han ( はん ) Solo. Han Solo is the only man strong and brave enough to put an end to this snake and his crime filled ways.
readings.hint: Now see Han Solo doing battle with the snake (cue Star Wars music). The snake hisses and lunges at Han, but Han shoots the snake and it’s incapacitated. Smell the smell of scorched snake flesh in your nose.

meanings.mnemonic: You slip off the roof , your life flashes before your eyes, and you smash your mouth on the ground causing some serious damage . Oof, this is a really bad injury , you fell all the way off the roof and onto your mouth.
meanings.hint: Imagine that moment as you fall off the roof. In slow motion you can see the roof moving away, you see yourself as a little kid, a teen, an adult, then BAM you've injured your mouth. Ow ow ow!

readings.mnemonic: As you try to assess the damage , you hear someone shout, " Guy ( がい ), hey, you okay, guy?" They run up to you, but your mouth is too damage d, you can't respond. "Okay guy, I'm gonna call you Guy now. Let's get you to the hospital."
readings.hint: No matter how much you try to wave your hands and gesture, they keep calling you Guy. It's really annoying, actually, because you aren't a guy and you aren't Guy either. But your mouth is too damaged for you to tell him.

meanings.mnemonic: What you say is righteousness , at least that's what you believe. You keep talking and saying these things, and you don't listen to anyone else's words. This is your style of deliberation and discussion .
meanings.hint: Picture yourself in a deliberation with some other people. They think what they're saying is right, and it drives you mad. Then you realize that you're doing the same thing.

readings.mnemonic: What are you in deliberation about? Whether or not to send some poor soul to the gui llotine ( ぎ ). "I think he should go," you say. "I don't think he should," says another. This deliberation is going nowhere...
readings.hint: Imagine how the deliberation concludes. Then, at the end, someone says "This deliberation is over! We have decided to _________" (in regards to the guillotine).

meanings.mnemonic: A Chinese turkey is difficult to find because they are so rare. Chinese turkeys are the rarest variety of turkey, so naturally, finding one is exceedingly difficult.
meanings.hint: Try and picture a Chinese turkey in your brain. Now imagine yourself trying to catch it, but you just can’t. It’s too difficult. The Chinese turkey is too elusive.

readings.mnemonic: Do you know the actual reason Chinese turkey are so difficult to find? It’s because they’re all made out of Naan ( なん ). Of course they’re elusive when they blend in so well with all the other Naan!
readings.hint: Picture yourself entering a bakery, frantically scouring through all the Naan in an attempt to find one that’s actually a Chinese turkey. "WHERE ARE THE TURKEYS?", you scream, to everyone else’s bewilderment. Those elusive, difficult to find Chinese Naan turkeys…

meanings.mnemonic: A flood and a fire ! At the same time? This is a disaster !
meanings.hint: Imagine a disaster so bad that there are floods AND fires. That's too much!

readings.mnemonic: This disaster was caused by your cy borg ( さい )! It's malfunctioning, running around leaking large amounts of water and breathing fire. I guess you didn't work out all the kinks before you let it roam wild.
readings.hint: You better figure out a way to stop your cyborg from causing more disasters like this. Fix it now, or you're gonna be in big trouble.

meanings.mnemonic: When a woman sees the Mona Lisa , she feels a strong feeling of dislike . All women dislike the Mona Lisa because of how much attention she gets.
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself feeling this strong dislike towards the Mona Lisa. She gets all the attention and you get none. It’s not fair and you’re upset.

readings.mnemonic: You dislike the Mona Lisa so much you hire Ken ( けん ) the samurai to cut her up.
readings.hint: Ken walks slowly toward the Mona Lisa, dislike shines in his eyes, but then! He falls for her too. No! Ken, destroy her!

meanings.mnemonic: If you put your mouth on a tree and try to eat it, you’re probably a distressed and troubled individual (unless you’re a woodchuck). You don’t need to eat this tree, you have plenty of food at home. Get your mouth off that tree you distressed child.
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself as this distressed and troubled person. Taste the taste of sap and bark in your mouth as you nom on the tree with all your might.

readings.mnemonic: You thought you were the most distressed and troubled person in this situation, but then you look up and see a very equally distressed and troubled con dor ( こん ). The tree you've been mouthing is its home. And now you've slobbered all over it, ruining this condor's day.
readings.hint: Picture a very distressed condor watching you munch your mouth all over its tree house. You've made a condor enemy this day.

meanings.mnemonic: The fly radical and the fly kanji are exactly the same! Awesome!

readings.mnemonic: When you fly , you generate a lot of he at ( ひ ) because you’re moving so fast through the atmosphere. Normally this heat would be a nuisance, but you’re a superhero so it doesn’t bother you. This is actually the reason you’re able to fly – you’re a superhero and super resistant to heat.
readings.hint: You’re flying through the air super fast. Feel the air rushing past you as heat builds up all over your body from rocketing through the air so fast.

meanings.mnemonic: A flowers net is upon your forehead in the evening . These appear on your head at night whenever you are having a dream .
meanings.hint: Watch yourself sleeping. Then, a net of flowers appear on your forehead as you start to dream.

readings.mnemonic: You dream about cows saying " moo ( む ), moo, moo!"
readings.hint: While still dreaming, you say "moo" out loud too. "Moooo!"

meanings.mnemonic: A narwhal sticks a stick into the dirt to see what might exist underneath it. Narwhals live in the sea, you see. So they're convinced nothing can exist anywhere else.
meanings.hint: Imagine seeing a narwhal sticking a stick, and maybe even its horn too, into the dirt on land. It needs to know if life exists outside of the ocean for sure.

readings.mnemonic: The narwhal can't find anything but it can't stop thinking about what could exist outside of its world. It pulls out a xy lophone ( ざい ) and starts tapping the keys with its horn. It plays a sad song about the meaning of life and whether existence is worth it.
readings.hint: Hear the plinky plink of the xylophone and think about what exists in this world. It's a sad song, you realize.

meanings.mnemonic: Put a net around a criminal to show their guilt . Criminals look like anyone else on the street, so you decide to tie them in nets to make sure people know who they're dealing with.
meanings.hint: Imagine throwing a net around someone you think, no, you know is guilty of doing something bad to you. It feels pretty nice.

readings.mnemonic: The guilt shows on the criminal's face. They smile, shrug, and pull a stolen xy lophone ( ざい ) out of their back pocket.
readings.hint: Woah! The xylophone is huge, how did they... well whatever, you definitely have proof that they're guilty now and the cops should be here any minute to take your criminal catch.

meanings.mnemonic: Rain causes a landslide that causes an earthquake !!! The earth doesn't just slide, it shake s and quake s.
meanings.hint: Picture this happening in real time. First it starts to rain really hard, then the ground starts to shift and run, then everything is shaking and quaking so violently it's an earthquake!

readings.mnemonic: The earthquake shakes you so violently that you fall over, banging your shin ( しん ) on the coffee table. The edge of the coffee table is very sharp, so it cuts into your shin pretty badly.
readings.hint: You grab your shin, but the earthquake shakes the coffee table toward you to hit your shin yet again! Ow ow ow stop!

meanings.mnemonic: You stand on the Cliff of Life and give birth to a child. Oh no! You’re standing too close to the edge of the Cliff of Life! The baby falls off the cliff right after you give birth to it! In your despair, you throw all your property off the cliff too.
meanings.hint: The baby is falling off the cliff! Imagine the sheer terror you feel as the baby plummets towards the ground below.

readings.mnemonic: You give birth , and now this happens. Such bad luck. But wait! What's that sound? Sleigh bells? Could it be?! It's San ta-san ( さん )! He caught your baby! And all your property .
readings.hint: Oh Santa-san. You're always here for me when I need you most. You can keep all my property and give it away as presents.

meanings.mnemonic: The leader 's mouth has grown old from saying so many things. This is what makes them different from everyone else, they speak truly like an individual .
meanings.hint: Imagine that leader's old mouth saying things that make them stand apart from the others. They're individual from everyone else.

readings.mnemonic: As an individual , they must teach all the 子 s ( こ ) their way. Their individual way.
readings.hint: "You must be your own individual. Don't be sheeple," he says to the 子 s.

meanings.mnemonic: Under any cliff , there's some dirt that's under a lot of pressure .
meanings.hint: Imagine being down there under that heavy cliff. Imagine feeling the cliff's pressure as you watch it compress the dirt. It's so hard to breathe... h... help!

readings.mnemonic: You want to increase the pressure , so you stack on a second cliff. " Ah, two ( あつ )?!" the dirt cries out in dismay. This dirt is tough, but it's really starting to feel the pressure now.
readings.hint: Imagine yelling out to the dirt to encourage it: "Ah, two cliffs is nothing! Come on, you got this!" You're the dirt's gym buddy, and you won't let it buckle under the pressure.

meanings.mnemonic: Someone stabbed the leader mole with a knife to overthrow it. Now there is a new mole leader. Because that's what happens when you overthrow someone—someone else needs to take over. In this case, a vengeful mole.
meanings.hint: Imagine a mole sneaking up behind the leader mole and stabbing him with a knife. This type of overthrowing of leadership feels familiar to you. Maybe this has happened before in the past?

readings.mnemonic: Where do leaders get overthrow n the most? In とう きょう . Just think back in Japanese history, back when everyone was at war with one another. People were taking over and getting overthrown and then that guy gets overthrown and then THAT guy gets overthrown. Basically, it used to be hard to stay a leader in とうきょう .
readings.hint: Picture this like an exaggerated reenactment on the History channel. Leader after leader of とうきょう gets overthrown and replaced with the next guy. It never stops. (Until the end of the Edo period, I guess.)

meanings.mnemonic: Tying a thread to a tombstone each year will help you manage the passage of time . That way you’ll know how many years you pass through , since you’ll have one thread for each year to count.
meanings.hint: Picture yourself coming back to the same tombstone each passing year and tying a new thread to it. You're trying to manage these feelings by keeping track of how much time passes.

readings.mnemonic: Dealing with the passage of time can be tough, so after you tie the thread to the tombstone, each year you eat a piece of ca ke ( けい ). You already eat cake to mark the passage of time on your birthday, so why not here as well?
readings.hint: Maybe this is your future tombstone, and eating cake each year is a preemptive celebration of your death day! Ooh, what a spooky theory.

meanings.mnemonic: Next to a tree , you spot poop , not once but twice. Only a drunkard would be the type of person to not use the nearby machine toilet. There's a perfectly good outhouse with a machine toilet right there, but this drunkard is so drunk that he decides to poop under the tree instead. And not only does he poop once, he poops twice.
meanings.hint: Imagine this drunkard, squatting down under the tree to poop. You can smell the poop and a faint stench of alcohol in the air. How could he not have realized that there was a machine toilet right there?

readings.mnemonic: Oh, I see — the door to the outhouse with the machine toilet is locked, and the drunkard doesn't have the key ( き ). If he had a machine like a jackhammer, or a circular saw, or some other heavy duty machine, he might be able to get in without a key, but since he has none of those or a key, he's reduced to pooping under the tree instead.
readings.hint: Listen to the drunkard grumbling about the lack of a key as he poops under the tree. He doesn't sound happy, and every time he stresses to push out the poop, his voice fluctuates. Imagine hearing this in your mind and being a wee bit disgusted.

meanings.mnemonic: Sitting on the lid of a toilet, you're praying your poop explosion will be flushed away, beseeching the cross decorating the bathroom wall. The poop needs to be one hundred percent flushed away because you're on a date, and you don't want even one percent of your explosion left for them to see.
meanings.hint: You take a peek under the lid to check, and your poop explosion is one hundred percent gone! Phew!

readings.mnemonic: It was extra important that there was zero percent poop left, because you're not on a date just anywhere, you're at the Ritz ( りつ )!
readings.hint: Look around you at the classy surroundings of the Ritz Hotel bathroom. You feel one hundred percent relieved now, and ready to enjoy your date at the Ritz!

meanings.mnemonic: If there is a building in the direction you want to go it will prevent you from going any further. Like, come on kid, you think you can just walk right through a building? Don't make me laugh!
meanings.hint: There's a building right there. Try to go that direction. Oh, did the building prevent you? Told you so.

readings.mnemonic: The building is prevent ing you from going the right direction, so you pull a bow ling ( ぼう ) ball out of your bag, aim, and bowl the building over. The building, and all the ones around it, go tumbling down. But now the rubble is preventing you from going further.
readings.hint: Your bowling antics are now preventing a bunch of people from going the directions they want to go. Nice job.

meanings.mnemonic: You walk into this tree and hit your head on it, then a knife falls out of it to stab you. Ow! What the heck?! What was a knife doing in that tree?
meanings.hint: Imagine the pain and confusion of being stabbed by a violent tree. You hit your head on the tree again, as if to test it, and again a knife falls out and stabs you. Oh, so it's a stabbing tree, is it? That does it.

readings.mnemonic: There's nothing more dangerous than a tree that likes to stab , so you get your saw ( さ ) and start to saw it down. Trees that stab get sawed.
readings.hint: Feel the grain of the saw's wooden handle in your bare hand. As the saw bites into tree bark, a retaliatory rain of knives comes stabbing down around you. But you don't even flinch. You will saw this stabbing tree down.

meanings.mnemonic: Wash your self with a big bar of soap if you're stinking everything up.
meanings.hint: Seriously. If you or someone else is stinking to high heaven, embrace a big bar of self care.

readings.mnemonic: You're stinking up the room because you spent your day rolling in 草 ( くさ ). And based on this particular stench, I'd say an animal left something in that 草 too...
readings.hint: Imagine trying to use a big bar of soap on yourself to get the stinky 草 off. But it's not working... the 草 stink is SO STRONG!

meanings.mnemonic: You see a flag stuck in the ground with a little bit of fur coming out from it and on closer inspection you realize it’s actually a tail . Someone stuck a flag into some sort of animal, pushing it under the ground. All that’s visible now is some fur and part of the tail.
meanings.hint: What kind of animal could it be? You start to feel the soft fur with your hand and then the stench of death hits your nostrils. Ew.

readings.mnemonic: You try to pull the flag out of the ground and hear a rumbling buzzing sound. At the end of the flagpole you see a huge bee hive ( び )! These bees made their home under the tail ! Maybe that’s why the flag was sticking in it… to warn people. Oops.
readings.hint: See the bees swarming out from around the tail and toward your face. Run! Run before there’s nothing left of you but your own tail!

meanings.mnemonic: Regardless of whether or not you wear a hat while you jackhammer , you’re going to have a surplus of noise. Jackhammers are loud, and your hat is doing nothing to dampen the racket. There’s a surplus of commotion coming from the jackhammer and onlookers are not amused.
meanings.hint: The hat you’re wearing is ridiculous, giving you a surplus of bad style. Imagine a bunch of people scowling at you, not only because of your crazy hat, but because of the annoying sound of the jackhammer. Hear the obnoxious noise around you as the jackhammer rattles your bones.

readings.mnemonic: One of the people walking by is the owner of a push cart yo -yo ( よ ) stand. Having a surplus of yo-yos, he starts throwing them at you. This makes you stop using the jackhammer as you need both hands to fend off the yo-yo barrage.
readings.hint: Your hat is doing little to defend you from the onslaught of yo-yos being tossed at you. Feel the yo-yos hitting your body and listen to them roll away after striking you.

meanings.mnemonic: You say , throwing your hat on the ground in front of the bookshelf , "I have a theory !" All this reading and thinking has finally given you a wonderful idea, a theory you think might solve all of your problems!
meanings.hint: This theory you've thought of is great, it's going to change everything!

readings.mnemonic: "What is your theory, Ron ( ろん )?" asks Hermione. You're Ron Weasley, and you just came up with a theory that you think will help you stop Voldemort.
readings.hint: "Ron? Ron, what's your theory? What's your theory, Ron?" Hermione asks over and over. But you freeze. You don't remember who you are. "Hermione, I have a theory that I was just a person learning Japanese, and not a magical boy at all." "Shut up Ron, that's a stupid theory," she replies.

meanings.mnemonic: Hanging off the edge of a cliff is a sun -worshipping child with a thick unibrow. Hanging off the cliff, the child cries out to the sun to save them from this cruel fate. Even the thickness of their unibrow is not enough to give the child the strength to climb back up on their own.
meanings.hint: I mean, this unibrow is really, really thick. So thick it doesn’t even look real. But it is. It’s really real and really thick.

readings.mnemonic: The child glances up at their thick unibrow and yells, " ah, two ( あつ )!" Yup, it turns out the sun misunderstood the child's pleas for help. Instead of pulling them up from the cliff, it gave them a second thick unibrow on top of the first one.
readings.hint: Imagine the sun noticing its mistake and apologizing to the child. "Ah, two thick unibrows wasn't what you wanted? Wow, I'm sorry. This is embarrassing… I'm actually not sure I can remove the second one now that it's on there."

meanings.mnemonic: You place a cross on a Wolverine woman . She's gone now, but she was once your wife .
meanings.hint: Oh, how you miss your Wolverine woman and her long, sharp claws… Now all you've got left to remember her by is this cross. Your poor, poor wife.

readings.mnemonic: Wait a minute… She can't be dead because your wife is a cy borg ( さい )! How could you forget?!
readings.hint: All your wife needs is a little cyborg tune-up. Bionic brain here, some new claws there. Yep, your cy-wife's as good as new.

meanings.mnemonic: The life of a shellfish is filled with blame . They take the blame for everything from food poisoning and allergies, to cutting people's feet on beaches.
meanings.hint: Just imagine an innocent little shellfish, living its quiet little life, while everyone's heaping blame on it. It hides in its little shell and tries to deflect all that blame. What a life!

readings.mnemonic: When those poor shellfish aren't having blame piled on them, they're being eaten, because shellfish are thought to make for a very sexy ( せき ) meal.
readings.hint: Imagine trying to look sexy as you slurp up those saucy little shellfish.

meanings.mnemonic: You see a tsunami that's simultaneous (with another tsunami, I assume) and you know the world is about to come to an end .
meanings.hint: Imagine a tsunami simultaneously happening as the world comes to an end. Oh no, isn't there any way to stop this!?

readings.mnemonic: Just as the world is about to come to an end , your cy borg ( さい ) appears and blocks it with its cyborg body!
readings.hint: So the world won't come to an end this one time, thanks to the cyborg you made. Though it doesn't look very good. The cyborg is beaten up and full of water. This might be the end for it...

meanings.mnemonic: Your magic yoga brush lets you build what's in your mind's eye.
meanings.hint: You do the bridge pose, and in your mind you imagine your brush painting an actual bridge. When you open your eyes, there it is! A bridge has been built, leading straight to your front door.

readings.mnemonic: You build a bridge. Immediately after, someone is climbing up onto it. It's Ken ( けん ) the samurai. He waves at you and says, "Did you build this? The craftsmanship is amazing."
readings.hint: It seems like Ken the samurai really appreciates your building skills.

meanings.mnemonic: A winter tree is only a winter tree due to a clause in its tree contract.
meanings.hint: The clause states the tree can only be a tree in winter, but not during other seasons. Once winter descends, the clause takes effect and it becomes a tree. (See how winter is descending on the tree from above in this kanji?) Go ahead and look at its contract — see the winter tree clause, right there, in fine print.

readings.mnemonic: This clause was written into all trees' contracts by Joe ( じょう ), the large farmhand.
readings.hint: Joe noticed that the trees were getting too crowded, so he wrote up these clauses and added them to the trees' contracts. Now, the forests are less crowded, thanks to the little clause. Makes sense that a farmhand like Joe would do this, too. The fewer trees, the more farmland. It's good for folks like Joe.

meanings.mnemonic: Beside your mouth you hold your cross as you pray for all your wishes to come true . Perhaps some divine power will grant what you wish for?
meanings.hint: Picture yourself holding your cross right up next to your mouth as you speak your prayers. What will you wish to come true? It could be anything you want!

readings.mnemonic: And if you really want your wish to come true , make sure to write it down in kana !
readings.hint: Legend has it that writing down your wishes in kana will lead to higher chances of them actually coming true! Whether you go with hiragana or katakana is up to you, just make sure it's all in some form of kana.

meanings.mnemonic: A grain woman is a woman who talks about grain with other women on a committee . Together they're known as the Committee of Grain Women. What does this committee discuss, you wonder.
meanings.hint: Imagine what this committee of women could be coming together to discuss. Isn't grain just grain? Why do they need a whole committee?

readings.mnemonic: Well, they formed this committee because ea gles ( い ) have been eating all the grain. The women meet as a committee and try to come up with ways to stop these pesky eagles from eating all the grain and leaving none for the humans.
readings.hint: Imagine a committee of women standing together in a field of grain, watching eagles swooping around. They take notes and mutter to one another about their options.

meanings.mnemonic: You only have a few eye s so you need to conserve what's left.
meanings.hint: You only have like, three eyes left. If you conserve them.

readings.mnemonic: You're trying to conserve your eyes, when the Shou gun ( しょう ) marches by. He stops, sees you, and demands you give him your remaining eyes! But sir, I'm conserving what little I have left!
readings.hint: The Shougun stares down at you, eyes blazing, and you hand over all but one of your eyes. Now you really have to conserve, or you'll be blind. Thanks Shougun. I bet he has plenty of eyes, he didn't need yours.

meanings.mnemonic: Cow s, using nothing more than a towel and a knife , have taken control of the world. Their system of control is brutal — payback for how we humans have treated them, I guess.
meanings.hint: Imagine this new cow-controlled world — there are cows on every street corner, armed with towels and knives, intimidating all us humans into following their system.

readings.mnemonic: It's time to take control back from the cows! Pull out your sa ber ( せい ) and fight back!
readings.hint: The cows' towels and knives won't stand a chance against your saber!

meanings.mnemonic: If you use your fingers to point when you compare things you're really just throwing around criticism . You should never need to point fingers when you're just comparing things. If you're pointing and picking and being mean, that's criticism.
meanings.hint: Some criticism is okay, as long as it's constructive and it helps the person get better. But when you start pointing fingers, it's time to step back and try to not be such a jerk.

readings.mnemonic: If you're having trouble not giving out criticism , just think about how you would react if someone criticized you. You'd feel an embarrassed he at ( ひ ) rise throughout your body. That heat will cover your whole face.
readings.hint: Before you throw criticism around, imagine that embarrassed heat. You don't want to feel that way, so why would you do it to someone else?

meanings.mnemonic: A lion harvesting rice uses an axe to cut off the tops of the stalks. The lion is roaming the rice fields using his axe to harvest the rice. He does so by cutting off the part of the rice stalks he needs.
meanings.hint: Picture this lion on his hind legs, walking through a rice field. He’s holding an axe in one hand and acting like a human. See him cut off the plants and hear the whoosh of the axe as he cuts.

readings.mnemonic: The lion will cut off rice stalks until dawn ( だん ). But at the first light of dawn, it will run and hide in the shade of the trees, cut off from the rice.
readings.hint: When dawn comes in Africa (it's a lion, so that's where he is) it gets too hot to be in the sun, so the lion gets cut off from his job of cutting things off.

meanings.mnemonic: Both a leader and a king have a duty to protect their people. A good leader always protects his followers, and a good king always protects his subjects. This is their duty.
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself being protected by your king and leader. They don’t really like you, but it is their duty to protect you. See them fighting off evil doers and taking bullets for your sake.

readings.mnemonic: You’re the village nin compoop ( にん ), but the king and the leader still have a duty to protect you. Even a nincompoop is important to the kings and leaders of the world.
readings.hint: You’re a nincompoop. Even though you’re so nincompoopy, your king and leader still have a duty to protect you. Feel happy and content, despite your nincompoopery.

meanings.mnemonic: The life thread is an element essential to all living things. This thread of life joins all things together, providing an essential relationship between all life. This element is all that holds this world together.
meanings.hint: Visualize this element of life, interconnecting all living things. Without it, everything would fall apart and into chaos.

readings.mnemonic: You need to keep this element clean, so you make sure to wash it with so ap ( そ ) every single day. This turns out to be pretty hard because the element is an invisible thread, but you do your best.
readings.hint: Imagine grabbing hold of the element that holds all life together and trying to wash it with soap. It's slippery so you keep dropping it and soap keeps going in your eyes.

meanings.mnemonic: Sheep are always looking at the next animal, full of envy and jealousy .
meanings.hint: Picture a sheep comparing itself to the next creature, and feeling deep envy and jealousy. Why does it feel that way?

readings.mnemonic: The reason the sheep feels such envy and jealousy is that the next creature is a cen taur ( せん )! Who wouldn't be jealous of a creature as noble as a centaur?
readings.hint: Being a centaur is every sheep's dream, so they can't help feeling terrible jealousy when they see one.

meanings.mnemonic: Seeing a mohawk in the winter means the enemy is nearby. You are from the dreadlock tribe and the mohawk tribe is your enemy. You’re deep in the heart of battle and winter has fallen. Now is the time to take out the enemy.
meanings.hint: Picture yourself holed up in a winter encampment, waiting to strike at the mohawk wearing enemies. Feel the cold around you as you scan the horizon for mohawks.

readings.mnemonic: You're kind of a techie ( てき ), so you have an advantage over your enemy . You're armed with night vision goggles and other cool tech gear as well. Being a techie certainly has its advantages.
readings.hint: Imagine yourself all teched out like the techie you are. Hear the beeps and boops of all the technology you have at your disposal as you search for more mohawk enemies.

meanings.mnemonic: If you say a new weapon idea out loud, then it will be establish ed immediately.
meanings.hint: Try it out: I want a stabbing weapon with extra weapons that can pop out on the sides. *POOF* You've established a new weapon. And it's been added to the official weapon wiki of the world.

readings.mnemonic: Weapons have to be establish ed in sets ( せつ ). So you have to make another one that goes with it. Make them cohesive please.
readings.hint: Establish weapons in sets or the people who use them won't be able to match each other.

meanings.mnemonic: You see a tree with a squid on it, so you get closer to examine it.
meanings.hint: As you examine the tree and the squid in it, you think, "Huh, nope still weird." You should examine it further to find out what's going on.

readings.mnemonic: You're sticking your face in really, really close to examine this little squid in the tree when Ken ( けん ) the samurai appears behind you. "There you are, squid! Thank you citizen, I've been looking all over for my little chum."
readings.hint: Imagine how many trees Ken examined before he found you both. Picture him putting his face really close to each one, then shedding a single tear when he realized this squid wasn't in it.

meanings.mnemonic: You have a scene going on inside your head . It is of a pair of horns on the ground atop a mountain . No. It is not just one pair of horns. It is many. So many that they create a giant hill atop the mountain, which is already pretty high.
meanings.hint: Imagine this hill of horns on top of this mountain. This hill of horns makes it possible for you to get just high enough to touch the top of the sky.

readings.mnemonic: How was standing atop this hill of horns on top of a mountain to touch the sky? Eh. It was oka y ( おか ), but that's about it.
readings.hint: Feel indifferent about this hill of horns on top of the mountain. It's all in your head anyways.

meanings.mnemonic: The dirt mask is a mask which, when put on, will greatly increase the amount of dirt.
meanings.hint: Imagine putting it on, and suddenly the dirt around you increases by quite a bit.

readings.mnemonic: Use the dirt mask to increase the dirt around you. The zo mbies ( ぞう ) are trying to escape, so you just keep increasing the dirt on top of them so they can't get out.
readings.hint: Zombie hands keep coming out of the ground, but as long as you wear the mask and increase the dirt on top of them, they won't be able to escape their earthly prison.

meanings.mnemonic: If a tree is growing out of a top hat you need to inspect it to find out why and how this happened. You should probably investigate the area too. I mean, how could a tree grow out of a top hat anyway? Seems suspicious.
meanings.hint: Inspect the tree. Inspect the top hat. Did you find anything?

readings.mnemonic: As you inspect the tree, you pick up a saw ( さ ) and start cutting it. You need to investigate how the tree and the top hat are connected, and a saw is the only way to do it properly.
readings.hint: Turns out the top hat was just decorative and the tree was growing out of the earth, like any other tree. Imagine your shame now. You should be more careful — don't be sawing trees willy nilly.

meanings.mnemonic: I hereby cut your assets in half with a knife , says the judge . At someone's divorce proceedings. Ah, the oldest judge trick in the book.
meanings.hint: If you can't decide who gets custody of the baby, the dog, the house, the car, the furniture, the savings, and the house plants, you have to leave it up to the judge. And the judge's judgment is that everything is cut in half with a knife. What an efficient judge.

readings.mnemonic: This judge is Han ( はん ) Solo, so you know he's not going to back down.
readings.hint: Han Solo the judge is a very busy person, so he has to judge quickly and efficiently. He'll cut all your stuff perfectly in half before he wastes time listening to you argue about who earned what. Sure it's crude, but at least it's… equal?

meanings.mnemonic: Building s hold festival s on special occasion s.
meanings.hint: A festival can't be happening all the time. Then it wouldn't be special, it would just be a party building, so they can only happen on special occasions.

readings.mnemonic: The occasion this time is to celebrate your wonderful cy borg ( さい ). It's about time you did something nice for it.
readings.hint: Your cyborg has been really great lately. Your wife loves it. It saved the world. It's nice of you to take this occasion to show the cyborg how much it means to you all with a festival.

meanings.mnemonic: The leader of the cow tribe is too important to deal with small matter s and trivial affair s.
meanings.hint: Picture this hoity-toity cow leader who thinks he’s too good for small matters and affairs. See him acting all fancy like he’s better than everyone else. The cow even moos with a fake British accent. How pretentious.

readings.mnemonic: All of his affair s are taken care of by Ken ( けん ) the samurai, who he hired as his underling.
readings.hint: Picture Ken the samurai scooping poop for the leader of the cows, because that's a matter of poop and state.

meanings.mnemonic: If you see a spirit with an axe , you better pray for your life. Spirits only hold axes if they're thinking about murder, so pray as hard as you can to try and get it to spare you.
meanings.hint: Imagine trying to pray as hard as you can as a spirit with an axe closes in on you. Can you hold your prayer concentration long enough?

readings.mnemonic: You pray and pray and pray and nothing happens. You stop praying and dare to open your eyes. There is a key ( き ) in front of you. Grab the key and hold it before you and pray for the spirit to take it. Moments later, both the key and the spirit are gone.
readings.hint: I guess the key was prayer all along. ( ͡° ͜ʖ ͡°)

meanings.mnemonic: Whatever people say to you will be carved by a blade into your heart . Sounds cruel, but it's a way to make you recognize their power.
meanings.hint: How could you not acknowledge somebody if every word they said was carved into your heart? It's not particularly painful, but it does make you quite uncomfortable.

readings.mnemonic: Having their words carved into your heart makes you recognize the authority of many people. But the only one you could ever truly acknowledge , is Nin tendo ( にん ).
readings.hint: You're one of the biggest Nintendo fans out there, so how could you not acknowledge their greatness? No matter what is carved into your heart, Nintendo will always occupy a special place.

meanings.mnemonic: If there’s grass on the ground and fins in your hand , you need to raise that hand up to the sky. You’re in the middle of a fisherman’s ritual, which can only be performed on grassy ground. You take fish fins from the previous year, and raise them up to the sky in order to ensure a good fishing harvest.
meanings.hint: Feel the grass in your toes and the fins in your hand as you raise them up to the sky. See the sun glistening off the fins as you raise them up in the air.

readings.mnemonic: This ritual only works in one part of the world however. It absolutely must be performed in Little Kyo to ( きょ ). The way Little Kyoto lines up with the sun and the sky and all that astrological junk makes it the best place for you to raise fish fins up in the air.
readings.hint: Imagine yourself going through the same process as before, only this time imagine yourself in Little Kyoto. Little Kyoto is really little, so make sure you're in the right spot.

meanings.mnemonic: All the world 's shellfish are yours to receive .
meanings.hint: They're all for you. You're the king of the shellfish. Look at all of them, the entire world's shellfish, and they're all yours to receive.

readings.mnemonic: Why do you want to receive all these shellfish? Because it's the mora l ( もら ) thing to do. Oh benevolent, moral king of all the world's shellfish, please receive us.
readings.hint: Picture yourself as some enlightened, moral king of all the world's shellfish, with your sole duty to receive and take in all the world's shellfish into your flock.

meanings.mnemonic: The kanji and the radical are exactly the same. That means they share meanings as well. Righteousness is as righteousness does, right?
meanings.hint: Go on, put that righteous radical knowledge to use!

readings.mnemonic: Such righteousness ! Well, at least according to the king who thinks he's being righteous. His version of righteousness is to send anyone who disagrees with him to the gui llotine ( ぎ ).
readings.hint: "THIS IS NOT RIGHTEOUSNESS!" you yell, just before the guillotine comes down on your neck.

meanings.mnemonic: The next shellfish will be yours, but this shellfish will be mine. These are the resources we have to share and distribute.
meanings.hint: Our resources are based around a system of trading shellfish. It's not that weird, plenty of ancient societies used shellfish as their main resource!

readings.mnemonic: You save up all of your resources so you can buy a shee p ( し ). You bring a big sack of your shellfish resources to the market and finally get to pick out that sheep you've had your eyes on for months.
readings.hint: Of course you're going to use your resources to get a sheep. You're living in a time when shellfish are used to trade for goods and services!

meanings.mnemonic: Under a tree , a broken arrow protrudes from a turkey . It was fighting for the rights of its fellow turkeys, and was mortally wounded in the process.
meanings.hint: Imagine comforting this turkey as it leans against the tree, breathing its final breaths. "I lay down my life for the rights of my people…" it gasps. What a noble turkey.

readings.mnemonic: "You have rights , so I won't shoot you like this turkey," says the voice of Ken ( けん ) the samurai, from above you in the tree.
readings.hint: Ken jumps down and picks the turkey up, the broken arrow sticking out of its neck. "Turkeys do not have rights, my friend. Just let this one go."

meanings.mnemonic: Some of your grain has just been taken by a guard . This is the tax you pay for living in this town. Back in the day, grain was used like currency, so it makes sense that guards went around collecting grain tax.
meanings.hint: Can you imagine a guard coming to your door and demanding you pay your grain tax?

readings.mnemonic: You have to pay this tax , because if you don't the guard will cut you down with his special German saber, a zei ber ( ぜい ). "Don't you hear vat I zei? Pay ze tax or I vill zlei you vis my zeiber!" he shouts.
readings.hint: You don't want to be sliced with the zeiber, so you listen to vat he zei-zei-zeis and pay the tax.

meanings.mnemonic: You use a special spear in winter to give yourself the power to do all of your task s.
meanings.hint: It's hard to get a task done in winter. That's why you have this spear to power and motivate you.

readings.mnemonic: Your task is to spear this cow. " Moo ( む )!" it yells at you and runs away. You need to make dinner though... that's your task, so you better catch up to that cow and put an end to its mooing.
readings.hint: The moo haunts you as you finish your task. Luckily this spear is helping you keep going even though this task is really difficult.

meanings.mnemonic: Taking out your thread , you sew together the public 's heart s to make them whole again.
meanings.hint: The world has been in turmoil and the public has been suffering, but it's time to stop splitting our hearts apart and fighting. It's time to be whole again.

readings.mnemonic: Once their hearts are whole , the public's sou l ( そう ) becomes one!
readings.hint: Suddenly people who were fighting for years are crying and holding one another. Their souls recognize each other, they're healed, and the whole nation is finally seeing eye to eye.

meanings.mnemonic: A tsunami is coming for the cliff made of tofu , but there is a special sect that protects this tofu cliff.
meanings.hint: This sect of the tofu-lovers club takes their duties very seriously and they must protect this tofu cliff at all costs.

readings.mnemonic: All of the members of the sect are standing on the cliff, their hands raised, and right as the tsunami is about to collide with them, they shout, " Ha ( は )!" and it's repelled! The tsunami sinks and rolls back out to sea, the tofu cliff is safe!
readings.hint: Oh, do you want to be a member of the tofu sect? HA! Don't make me laugh. If you ask to be a member of a sect, you'll never get in.

meanings.mnemonic:
You use your fingers in the game of football to aid and assist the other players. And when you smash your fingers in football, you need to get some aid yourself.

If you're from the UK, just imagine someone kicking or stomping on your fingers instead.
meanings.hint: Picture yourself playing football. You catch the ball with your fingers and then assist your team by running and smashing your body into the enemy team. But in the process you smash your fingers... so you need to go to the aid tent.

readings.mnemonic: When you need aid for your football wrecked fingers you can't help but feel like it's the en d ( えん ) for you. Your fingers hurt so much, you don't even want the nice nurses to aid you.
readings.hint: You can't assist your team anymore because you got hurt. This isn't fair! It's the end. The end of your life and your career. Boo hoo hoo!

meanings.mnemonic: You have a great ability because your heart gives you the appearance of a superhuman. Your heart is really strong and powerful, fuelling your body to the best of its ability. All this together gives you the appearance of someone in top condition.
meanings.hint: See yourself in this ideal appearance. Everyone loves to look at you because you look so awesome. Your heart has given you many great abilities. Feel strong and powerful in this body of yours.

readings.mnemonic: While you may have the appearance of someone who is super strong, powerful, and awesome, you do have one shortcoming. You don’t know how to tie ( たい ) a tie. Actually, you don’t even know how to tie your shoes, let alone your tie. How embarrassing.
readings.hint: Since you don’t know how to tie a tie or tie your shoes, you wear slip ons and clip ons. Hear the noise of Velcro being undone as you kick off your shoes and unclip your tie after a hard day’s work at the office.

meanings.mnemonic: The clothes radical and the clothes kanji are exactly the same! Surprising!

readings.mnemonic: An ea gle ( い ) sees a pile of your clothes and lays an e gg ( え ) in them. The eagle’s egg needs a soft warm place to incubate, and your pile of clothes is the perfect place.
readings.hint: Look at the eagle and her egg sitting atop your clothes. Every time you get close the eagle starts to screech at you fiercely. Screee! Screee!

meanings.mnemonic: They say yoga is the correct way to go through birth . Birth isn't easy, but people keep telling you that yoga is the right way to prepare and actually do it.
meanings.hint: Picture multiple people you know saying you should do yoga if you plan to give birth. It isn't a question of whether you want to, it's the correct way to do it. So you should.

readings.mnemonic: To prepare for giving birth you end up doing so much yoga outside that you get a really deep tan ( たん ).
readings.hint: Hopefully having a tan doesn't make birth harder, since you've worked so hard to do this the correct way with so much yoga.

meanings.mnemonic: See all those icicle s to the dog 's left? Good thing they all fell on his left, or he would've been in a terrible condition .
meanings.hint: Picture a bunch of big, sharp icicles all falling down directly next to this dog. Now imagine the condition he would've been in, had they all fallen directly on top of him. Poor little thing.

readings.mnemonic: What saved the dog's condition was none other than Joe ( じょう ), the local farmhand. He constantly keeps an eye on the dog's condition, including any dangerously sharp icicles that could descend on him.
readings.hint: Good guy Joe, always worrying about his dog's condition. Everyone needs a farmhand like Joe.

meanings.mnemonic: You use the power of your mouth to spit shellfish at people as a form of congratulations ! In fact, you yell "CONGRATULATIONS!" as the shellfish come pouring out of your powerful mouth.
meanings.hint: Shellfish were once used as currency, so this totally makes sense. Just think of the shellfish as money coming out of your mouth as you say your congratulations.

readings.mnemonic: When you say congratulations like this, you look just like a ga rgoyle ( が ). You're crouching down, your mouth wide open, just like a stone gargoyle.
readings.hint: The next time you're congratulating someone, assume the gargoyle stance and dump all of their congratulatory shellfish on them from your mouth.

meanings.mnemonic: Inside the building is the Winter Cow . You need to see it, so you go inside. It is deep down in there, and you'll have to descend to reach it.
meanings.hint: Focus on descending the stairs. There are many signs that say "To Winter Cow: descend" with an arrow pointing down. "I guess I'll have to descend," you say.

readings.mnemonic: You descend to the bottom and there it is, the Winter Cow. You walk up to the cow, and it swings its head back to see you. Instead of a cow's face, it's こう いち 's face.
readings.hint: "Hello, thank you for descending all the way down here to see me," says the Winter Cow with こういち 's face. "You thought you could use the Winter Cow to stop me, but as you can see, I got here first."

meanings.mnemonic: You use a bamboo saw to cut down tree s to construct and build a building. The bamboo saw was crafted by a master bamboosmith, and cuts through trees with ease so you can quickly construct this new building.
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself working the bamboo saw. It's made of bamboo, but its sharp edge glistens with a mysterious aura as you slice through tree after tree. Feel how unnaturally easy it is to cut down piles of logs to build with.

readings.mnemonic: As you construct and build this building, you hear a strange cheeping noise. Suddenly a chick ( ちく ) pops out of one of the logs you are building with. Then another, and another. You're surrounded by chicks! Is this because you used the magical(?) bamboo saw??
readings.hint: Imagine trying to construct the rest of this building covered in baby chicks. They're very cute, but this is dangerous! You have to build very carefully, so you don't accidentally hurt any of them.

meanings.mnemonic: You kiss each and every one of your best pals to show them how much you love and care for them. You kiss each friend right on the mouth.
meanings.hint: Imagine kissing each of your best friends on the mouth. Does this make you uncomfortable? Excited? Both? Feel that feeling.

readings.mnemonic: Actually, each and every one of your best pals is of the cock ( かく ) variety. By that, I mean each of them is a rooster. Imagine kissing each cock on its beak.
readings.hint: Kissing the cock beak is kind of a strange experience. Feel the feathers in your hands as you steady the cock to kiss it. Cock-a-doodle-doo!

meanings.mnemonic: You should say " peace " before you evaluate a situation. That way everyone will know where you’re coming from. If you’re honest and say "peace" before you evaluate a situation out loud, everyone will know where you stand.
meanings.hint: What situation would you like to evaluate? Be sure to say "peace" before doing so. Imagine yourself doing this out loud and think of what you would say.

readings.mnemonic: When you say "peace," you end up making a really bad pun and you hear somebody say “ Heyo !” ( ひょう ) You look at the people around you and try to evaluate who said heyo, but you can’t tell. You start talking again when somebody exclaims “That’s what she said” and another person shouts “Heyo!” once again, but you still can’t evaluate who’s saying these things.
readings.hint: Imagine yourself trying to explain a situation you’re about to evaluate and your audience keeps shouting “Heyo!” every other sentence. Imagine how frustrating that would be. Get angry at your rude audience and how much they shout “Heyo.”

meanings.mnemonic: Dollar s and shellfish are used to pay expense s and cost s. Shellfish used to be used as currency, so they're like the coins that go with dollars.
meanings.hint: Imagine opening your bank account and looking at all of your monthly expenses. Now drag the little dollar and shellfish icons over to cover all of those costs.

readings.mnemonic: What is the biggest expense and cost ? It's the hea t ( ひ ) bill.
readings.hint: When you see how expensive your heat is, you feel a heat going through your body too.

meanings.mnemonic: You have something to say , so you stand up, throw in the towel , and shout: "I GIVE UP !" The stand radical looks a little weird in this kanji, probably because you're all tired and ready to give up.
meanings.hint: Imagine standing up and throwing in the towel (or even just waving it around, especially if it's white) — it's the universal signal for giving up, right? Just make sure you also say it out loud, to make it clear for everyone: "I GIVE UP!"

readings.mnemonic: What's making you want to give up ? Well, there's a killer ( あきら ) behind you who's threatening to kill you if you don't.
readings.hint: Hey, whatever you're doing, it's not worth losing your life over. If a killer is threatening you, you should probably just give up for now and live to fight another day.

meanings.mnemonic: The womb is a circle of power where a force of nature is formed. The womb has the power to create life. Life is a force of nature, and the circle of life starts in the womb.
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself in the womb, the circle of power. You’re being born into the world. Feel the force of your mother all around you.

readings.mnemonic: Now pretend you're the mother. You'll need all the force you can muster to bring that baby into the world, so you grab hold of your special sa ber ( せい ) and slice it about in the air to give you the extra force you need.
readings.hint: All that force works wonders, and when the baby's born you slice through the umbilical cord with your saber.

meanings.mnemonic: “Bring in the next woman so that I may examine her figure !” We’re holding auditions for a commercial and we need to find a woman with a nice figure. The last woman was no good, so bring in the next one.
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself as a figure judge on this panel. You need to find a woman with the perfect figure to cast in your commercial. You'd cast yourself, but you just aren't confident (Ridiculous! You're special, and beautiful, and no matter who you are [yes you reading this right now] you should be proud of who you are!).

readings.mnemonic: The next woman comes in and you examine her figure . The figure you've been searching for! It just happens to belong to a shee p ( し ) woman. But she's soft, fluffy, and round. The perfect figure!
readings.hint: We should all try harder to have a figure like this stunning sheep woman!

meanings.mnemonic: You can use a jackhammer to indicate and show someone where the prize is buried. You buried the prize to keep it safe, but the jackhammer is great at unearthing it. Jackhammers do a great job and show where the treasure lies. They indicate quite clearly where one needs to go.
meanings.hint: Imagine using this jackhammer to show someone where the prize is buried. Feel the vibrations through your hands and the rest of your body.

readings.mnemonic: Actually, you're unearthing Je sus ( じ ). He’s the treasure. For your soul. He’s here to show you that Jesus is still a hip and cool guy and he wants to indicate to you that having faith in Jesus and God and stuff is super rad. Jesus is all like, “Who has two thumbs and wants to show you the way of the Lord? This guy.”
readings.hint: Picture Jesus getting all over the top and super preachy about his religious beliefs. He’s doing everything he can to indicate to the crowd that he’s the best, and show them how cool he is. Listen to Jesus preach to the crowd, desperate for their attention.

meanings.mnemonic: From the roof , an icicle falls and hits Wolverine in the forehead , and he has to go over to the stool to lie down . And eventually he goes to sleep .
meanings.hint: Picture this entire scene. The icicle falls in slow motion from the roof. It hits wolverine right in the middle of his forehead. He goes down. He has to lie down. He has to sleep.

readings.mnemonic: While wolverine sleep s, he makes a horrible neigh ing ( ね ) sound. It's like there's a giant horse in his mouth screaming to get out! NEIGH NEIGH!
