meanings.hint: The lady of the house will tolerate nothing less than excellence, so get ready to sweep and re-sweep this floor till you meet your lady's expectations.

readings.mnemonic: Instead of your name, the lady of the house just calls you " foo l" ( ふ ). You rarely meet her ladyship's lofty standards, so from her perspective you're just a silly nincompoop fool who can't get anything right.
readings.hint: "You there — fool. Come sweep this floor again." "Yes, m'lady — your humble fool lives to serve, m'lady."

meanings.mnemonic: Using a hat , four person s with a cross make an umbrella . The hat is big enough to be an umbrella for all four people, so it makes sense. All four of them are religious missionaries, so that's why they're holding the cross under the "umbrella."
meanings.hint: See these four people all under one makeshift umbrella. The giant hat is set atop the cross, which is what makes it kinda like an umbrella, and the four pairs of hands are all holding it upright.

readings.mnemonic: You know the Spanish phrase "Mi casa ( かさ ) es su casa"? Well, this is kind of like that, but only with a hat. And that hat is also an umbrella . Mi casa es yours too, because it's an umbrella and also a hat. This makes sense, trust me.
readings.hint: Imagine the four people in the umbrella hat again, only this time imagine them skipping along singing "Mi casa es su casa." It'll help you remember.

meanings.mnemonic: Only give grain s to a turkey if it's immature . Adult turkeys should be eating healthier things, like insects and grass. But immature turkeys don't care if they get fat, so they whine and whine and whine until you give them grains.
meanings.hint: Imagine an immature turkey complaining at you until you give it grains. Now it's taking selfies with the grains and posting pics to Instagram and hashtagging them #GRAINLIFE.

readings.mnemonic: Another thing immature turkeys (and people) love is chee se ( ち ). Most of us non-immature people and birds still like cheese, but if you didn't have an immature cheese phase as a kid or teen, you won't be able to like cheese as a mature old person.
readings.hint: Imagine a bunch of immature kids and turkeys eating cheese together.

meanings.mnemonic: Super poop is a super power you have in infancy . Those in infancy have the power to poop at will, whenever and wherever they please. Infancy poop power!
meanings.hint: Infants poop a lot. There's poop everywhere. The powerful aroma overwhelms you. Eww.

readings.mnemonic: In infancy , one eats a lot of soft foods, such as yo gurt ( よう ). Yogurt is the food item above all others that grants superior pooping power.
readings.hint: See this infant gobbling up all the yogurt he can get his grubby little hands on. Soon, he begins to poop all over the place. Hear the infant screaming, "Goo-goo! Yogurt poop power! I'm a baby! Wahhhh!!"

meanings.mnemonic: You stick horns in the ground using them as spikes and you line up the horns in a straight line. Horns are pretty much the same thing as spikes once you stick them in the ground, and you line these ones up precisely for optimal protection.
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself lining up these horns in the ground. The spikes are sharp and you poke yourself with one. Ouch. Need to be more careful as you line these up.

readings.mnemonic: As you line up these horns and stick them into the ground, you notice a bull munching on hay ( へい ) nearby. It's staring at the horns you're lining up. Somehow it feels like it's munching that hay angrily. You gulp, looking at its horn as it grabs another mouthful of hay, and you feel pretty guilty.
readings.hint: Imagine the hay in that bull's mouth as it chomps angrily, watching you line up horns in the ground. It probably wishes you were the hay.

meanings.mnemonic: If you know the radical meaning lovely , you know the kanji meaning lovely as well!
meanings.hint: You know what would make learning this kanji lovely? Studying your radicals.

readings.mnemonic: " Lovely " is the name of my ray ( れい ) gun. It's more lovely than any other thing.
readings.hint: Try to imagine feeling this way about your ray gun. It's so lovely.

meanings.mnemonic: Your leader is using a stick in the winter made of hair . This is the stick of mastering and repair .
meanings.hint: Imagine your leader holding this stick of hair in the winter, going around mastering all kinds of skills and repairing everything in sight. What a strange stick, giving even stranger abilities to its wielder.

readings.mnemonic: What's the key to mastering any skill? A stick and a shoe ( しゅう )!
readings.hint: Picture your leader again; stick in one hand, shoe in the other. He looks kind of threatening, like he's about to slap you with the stick and then throw the shoe at you. But that's what it takes for mastering certain skills. This is all for discipline’s sake.

meanings.mnemonic: The odd radical and the odd kanji are the same. They're both... really strange .
meanings.hint: Remember how odd this one is?

readings.mnemonic: You're odd too, just like this kanji. But you don't want anyone to know, so you lock away your strange habits with a key ( き ).
readings.hint: You only give this odd key to people you trust, so they can unlock your odd secrets and see how odd you are deep down.

meanings.mnemonic: Blood and hair and tofu make up the populace . Those are the three categories the entire populace fits into, anyway.
meanings.hint: Blood shows that all the people are related. Hair shows that we're all humans. Tofu is food to all. So, as you can see, those three words describe the entire populace.

readings.mnemonic: The entire populace deserves shoe s ( しゅう ). It's a basic thing all humans should have like flowing blood, hair (somewhere), and tofu to eat.
readings.hint: If you see people in your populace without shoes, it's your job as a fellow member of society to help them get some!

meanings.mnemonic: A woman is one of the few peculiar things in the world. Everything but a woman can be explained and made sense of. There are only a few truly peculiar things in this world, with woman being one of them.
meanings.hint: Imagine saying this to a woman. Try and imagine her face as you tell her how peculiar she is.

readings.mnemonic: But when you think about it, we're all a little peculiar. Me, you, myou ( みょう ) everybody!
readings.hint: Who's peculiar? Me and you. Me, you — we are peculiar! Peculiar, peculiar, peculiar!

meanings.mnemonic: A tsunami that's blue is pure . Some tsunami are other colors, like brown or red because they aren't pure, they'll full of trash and poop and other horrible things. But a blue tsunami is completely pure!
meanings.hint: Picture this pure blue tsunami. You can see every crystal clear drop of water in it.

readings.mnemonic: Seeing this pure blue tsunami inspires you to pull out your glowing blue sa ber ( せい )! As the pure water comes toward you, you take one, long slash!
readings.hint: SPLAASHH! The tsunami's pure water splits and it absorbed into your saber. Now it's pure too. And transparent!

meanings.mnemonic: A tsunami takes life , cuts it like a sword through thread , and leaves everything behind completely pure and clean . Everything is dead and gone, wiped clean off the face of the earth, thanks to the tsunami. It's back to the way it was before humans were here, completely undefiled.
meanings.hint: Picture the tsunami purifying the land of life, and in your mind see the sword cutting clean through the thread of life.

readings.mnemonic: After the tsunami pulls away, everything left is pure white. You hate this, so you run over and squirt ketsu p ( けつ ) all over the place to make it not so pure.
readings.hint: The area is SO pure that the ground absorbs the ketsup and now it's all pure... ketsup color.

meanings.mnemonic: A gold Wolverine swam in the water while his father made a record of it. Wolverine is gold in color because he just won the gold medal in swimming, so now he is gold. Way to go, Wolverine.
meanings.hint: See this gold Wolverine holding his gold medal after getting out of the water. His dad doesn’t want to forget anything about this precious moment so he makes a record of it. Imagine him scribbling furiously in his notebook.

readings.mnemonic: Since Wolverine is actually a mutant, he is soon disqualified from his gold medal. His father makes a record of this also. One angry fan threw a rock ( ろく ) at Wolverine afterwards. Then another threw a rock. And another rock. Before you knew it, everyone was throwing rocks at Wolverine. Wolverine’s dad didn’t step in to help though, he just kept keeping records.
readings.hint: Imagine a pitiful Wolverine crying on the ground as each rock hits him. All he wanted was some recognition from his father. Poor guy. Imagine poor Wolverine like this as his dad just looks on and writes in his notebook.

meanings.mnemonic: Grain s get blame d for how much they accumulate . If the grains accumulate too much then they make more work for the farmers. If they accumulate too little, then they're starving the people. Ugh, grains just can't win with you people. They're blamed for everything.
meanings.hint: Picture a pile of grains accumulating in size while a person watches. No matter how large the pile gets, the person keeps blaming them for something!

readings.mnemonic: Now the grains have accumulate d a ton, they're overflowing in their grain sacks, and the farmer freezes and says, "Now those are some sexy ( せき ) grains."
readings.hint: Is that why people become farmers? Because they think... accumulating a lot of grains is sexy? People are weird.

meanings.mnemonic: A rice paddy you'd want to kiss is one that would probably have a cool abbreviation for their name, like Ri'-Pad. Yeah, that's definitely a cool abbreviation!
meanings.hint: Try to imagine how cool and confident Ri'-Pad is, they probably grow and wear their rice in a cool and trendy way, like a middle part or something.

readings.mnemonic: An abbreviation like Ri'-Pad is sure to get a strong reac tion ( りゃく ) — some will find it a little pretentious, some will hate it, but most will probably think it's really cool.
readings.hint: Try to visualize the different reactions people have to the name Ri'-Pad. What reaction did you have? What face did you make when you saw it?

meanings.mnemonic: Under a roof , but still in the sun , a woman is laying out a fabulous banquet . The woman doesn't want anyone to burn from the sun, but banquets are best outside, so she's set up a roof to protect her and her guests.
meanings.hint: Picture a large banquet of food on a sunny day, covered by an outdoor roof, and a woman carefully setting it all up.

readings.mnemonic: Unfortunately, the banquet roof comes crashing down right before the guests arrive, and it end s ( えん ) before it can start.
readings.hint: Honestly though, have you ever been to a banquet that ended well? Something always goes wrong when you have that many people under one roof.

meanings.mnemonic: There’s a tsunami in heaven and you take a small drop of water and append it to the tsunami. Now the tsunami is big enough to destroy all of heaven. The small drop that you appended to the tsunami was just enough to push it over the edge, reaching full destruction capability.
meanings.hint: Take a small drop of water and append it to the tsunami. Feel the drop of water on your finger and visualize yourself flicking it at the tsunami as it races towards heaven.

readings.mnemonic: Just after you append the small drop of water to the tsunami, it grows ten ( てん ) stories high. Before it was only nine, but since you appended that drop, the tsunami is now a full ten stories tall.
readings.hint: Look up at this ten story tall tsunami. It looks big and powerful. Feel the water spray from the tsunami as it towers over you.

meanings.mnemonic: If you're a tree anti (someone who's against trees), then you won't mind if I turn all of them into board s! Hahaha!
meanings.hint: "Boards are much more useful than trees." – The Anti-Tree Association. Now picture this sign on a big machine that's cutting up all the trees into big boards.

readings.mnemonic: The Tree Lovers Association has one word when they see you turning trees into board s: " Ban ned ( ばん )," they shout at you. "Boards mean you're banned!"
readings.hint: You couldn't care less about getting banned from the Tree Lovers Association. You and your Anti-Tree Association still keep on turning every tree into boards, ban or no ban. Damn those trees!

meanings.mnemonic: In the dirt you place a cross and surround it with a net made of clothes to keep it safe, but this net tends to break . Of course it breaks, it's just some clothes you tied together.
meanings.hint: Imagine what this janky makeshift net would look like. It's both comical and sad. Every day you set it up around the cross, but next morning it's lying in a broken heap in the dirt. What keeps breaking it?!

readings.mnemonic: You realize what keeps making your net break are kay aks ( かい ). The cross and net are right next to a river trail that kayakers do their kayaking in, and every time they go by their paddles smack into your net and break it.
readings.hint: Imagine the rage you'd feel at these unrepentant kayakers who keep breaking your stuff. Oh, and don't forget to be angry at yourself — for putting the cross and net so close to a river that kayaks can't even go by without breaking it.

meanings.mnemonic: A good leader will use charcoal to mark her colleague s. Sometimes if leaders aren't around all the time, people will get rebellious and act badly toward people who can get them in trouble. So when you mark them with charcoal you know that someone close with your leader is around and you should probably stop being a butt.
meanings.hint: Imagine seeing one of the leader's colleagues walking around, watching you. You'd get back to work and stop goofing around, right?

readings.mnemonic: You have to be good around her colleague s, because if you aren't your row ( りょう ) boat permit will be taken away. Row boating is the only fun you ever get to have, besides learning kanji. So act as best you can around the leader's colleagues so you don't lose your row boat rights.
readings.hint: Imagine goofing off in your row boat and then seeing one of the colleagues waiting on the shore for you. No! She's gonna take your row boat away!

meanings.mnemonic: A tree is not a cup of liquid or a counter for cups . Don’t get them mixed up. The last time you tried making a cup out of a tree to count your throat got filled with splinters when you used it. There wasn't even any liquid in it.
meanings.hint: Imagine cutting a tree into a cup of liquid and focus on the pain in your throat when you try to actually use it. Instead you kick the tree and angrily count cups of liquid in your head, because they simply are NOT the same.

readings.mnemonic: Another way to remember the counter for cups using a cup of liquid would be by creating and memorizing a hai ku ( はい ) about them. Aren’t haikus useful?
readings.hint: Think of a haiku about a cup of liquid and a counter for cups. Use that haiku to help remember this kanji.

meanings.mnemonic: A woman who is good is my daughter . My daughter is a good woman, I guarantee it.
meanings.hint: Look at how good this woman is! She’s my daughter! She’s good! You wish your daughter was as good a woman as mine is!

readings.mnemonic: Your daughter , on the other hand, is not a good woman. Your daughter looks like a moose who may ( むすめ ) not be able to find its way out of a wet paper bag. Your daughter looks like a moose who may lose its way without a map. Your daughter looks like a moose who may do lots of stupid things.
readings.hint: Think about how your daughter is basically a moose who may do dumb things. How ashamed you feel. How very, very ashamed. Unlike how I feel about my wonderful, perfect, not a moose-like daughter.

meanings.mnemonic: They say to wear a hat to cover your hair if your doctor diagnose s you with hair loss. It's the best option, folks.
meanings.hint: Imagine the doctor diagnosing you with hair loss. He pulls out a hat and places it on your poor, thinning hair.

readings.mnemonic: You just had the doctor diagnose you with hair loss. How terrible! You stumble out of the room, dumbfounded. Not paying attention, you bang your shin ( しん ) on the table. Your shin really hurts. What an awful day.
readings.hint: Hold your shin as you feel the throbbing pain in it. Even though the pain is so extreme, you can’t help but still think about the diagnosis you got from the doctor.

meanings.mnemonic: Under this roof someone odd is trying to draw near to you. You back up, but they continue to draw near anyway.
meanings.hint: Imagine being under the same roof as an odd person who's always trying to draw near to you. You don't want anything to do with them, but they won't back off.

readings.mnemonic: They continue to draw near until you can't take it anymore. You run out from under the roof, slam the door closed, and lock it with a key ( き ).
readings.hint: You hold the key tightly in your hand and press your ear to the door. You can still hear the odd person drawing near. Hopefully this key keeps them away.

meanings.mnemonic: Above you on a small stool is the eye of the one who will direct and supervise you at work. He’s really high above you, so all you can see is the glint of his supervising eye. But you can hear him shouting directions clearly, telling you exactly what to do.
meanings.hint: Look up and see that eye, shining down and supervising you closely. Hear the man shouting as he directs you in one task after another. It makes you uncomfortable, honestly.

readings.mnemonic: A man who can direct others so well must be Toku gawa ( とく ) Ieyasu. Sure enough, when he finally comes down off his stool you see that he’s the ghost of Tokugawa Ieyasu!!! That explains why you could only make out the ghostly gleam of his supervising eye before.
readings.hint: Tokugawa lingers before you in full shogun garb, then nods his head in approval, winks, and disappears.

meanings.mnemonic: In the morning , you use a gun on your nose to make it dry .
meanings.hint: Don't worry, there aren't bullets in the gun. It's an air gun! So just imagine shooting the air into your nose every morning to make sure it's nice and dry.

readings.mnemonic: This technique for a nice dry nose was invented by Genghis Khan ( かん )! Well, not him, but someone who worked under him. You see, he had a bit of a damp nose problem.
readings.hint: Now the next time you manage to get your nose nice and dry, think back to our founding father Genghis Khan and thank him for where you are today.

meanings.mnemonic: A district that lack s things will be in Europe .
meanings.hint: Picture a map of Europe in your mind. Now picture little words saying which district is lacking what. This one is lacking water. That one is lacking food.

readings.mnemonic: Europe is lacking so much because their 王 ( おう ) keeps taking everything.
readings.hint: Okay, so maybe this is an old view of Europe. But back in the old days the 王 s there would take everything from their subjects, so every district would lack something. Good thing things aren't that way now, huh?

meanings.mnemonic: A saw cuts into your heart and you are filled with fear . Well, you were filled with fear before the saw reached your heart too, but now that the saw is cutting into your heart, you’re feeling more fear than ever.
meanings.hint: Try and imagine this saw slicing into your heart. Are you filled with fear? You should be.

readings.mnemonic: Even more so than your fear of dying, you are filled with the fear that you’ll never get to vacation in きょう と . You always wanted to take a trip to きょうと , but if you die from this saw wound to the heart, you’ll never get the chance to go.
readings.hint: I fear you’ll never make it to きょうと . Think of きょうと and how you long for it as you slowly die from that saw wound to the heart.

meanings.mnemonic: A tsunami rolls in and destroys all the construction that was being done on the inlet and the bay . Both the inlet and the bay were having a lot of construction done on them to repair the damage from the last tsunami, and then another tsunami rolled in and destroyed all that too. Bummer.
meanings.hint: Picture all the construction on the bay and the inlet getting destroyed by the tsunami. Look at all of it fall apart and crash to the ground as the tsunami knocks it down. Imagine yourself right near the action, feeling the water spray from the tsunami.

readings.mnemonic: Imagine the Japanese people standing on the bay and the inlet as the tsunami approaches. “ Eh ( え )? What is that, Akiko?” “Eh? Where? Oh gawd, it’s a tsunami!!”
readings.hint: EH? A tsunami you say? Those construction areas are screwed, man. Again, imagine the tsunami demolishing the area. Then be all like, “Eh? A tsunami? Aw, man.”

meanings.mnemonic: An animal eats a child from a plate in a very fierce manner. This is a fierce animal, so it makes sense that it would eat the child in a fierce style.
meanings.hint: See the fierce animal fiercely eating the child from the plate. It’s very gruesome. See blood and body parts flying everywhere as the animal devours the child.

readings.mnemonic: The reason this fierce animal is eating the child from the plate is because the child forgot to mow ( もう ) the animal’s lawn. The fierce animal had hired the child to mow his lawn today, and when the child forgot to do it, the fierce animal hunted the child down and ate him.
readings.hint: That child really shouldn’t have forgotten to mow the lawn. Imagine you are the child, being eaten by the fierce animal and wishing that you would have just mowed the lawn like you said you would.

meanings.mnemonic: In the morning , Korea will still be Korea .
meanings.hint: Picture Korea first thing in the morning. You can see all the cities, the countryside, everything waking up.

readings.mnemonic: You know who was a big fan of Korea ? Genghis Khan ( かん ). He liked Korea so much that he totally ignored it while he was subjugating the crap out of the rest of Asia.
readings.hint: Sadly for Korea, the later Khans wouldn't be quite as kind (looking at you, Ögedei, Güyük, and Möngke). They liked Korea too, but they showed their love a little more… aggressively.

meanings.mnemonic: The narwhal is meeting in private with the turkey so they can, uh, show each other some private things. Doing this they realize they're both male ! You gotta be pretty manly and brave to do something like that.
meanings.hint: Picture a male narwhal and a male turkey meeting together in private. Whispering. Giggling. Pointing at each other's male bits, each admiring how manly and brave the other is.

readings.mnemonic: These male animals are obviously experimenting with things still. Ah, they must be you ths ( ゆう )!  To be a youth, again!
readings.hint: It's just nature. Youths like to learn and try new things. And if two male youth animals wanna get together in private that's their business.

meanings.mnemonic: The radical means mysterious and the kanji means the same thing: mysterious . It can also mean dark , 'cause mysterious things tend to be kinda dark and shadowy.
meanings.hint: When's the last time you saw something bright and mysterious? Never, I bet.

readings.mnemonic: There is no one more mysterious than Gen ji ( げん ). He's tall, dark , handsome, and most of all... 〜 mysterious 〜 !
readings.hint: Who's the mysterious man who's always reciting poetry? Genji! Who's the mysterious man who gets all the ladies? Genji! Who's the mysterious man looking all dark and depressed because someone turned him down? G... Genji…

meanings.mnemonic: There’s an arrow in that turkey and you do everything in your power to recommend that we don’t eat the turkey. There’s an arrow in this turkey. The arrow looks a little broken, so there’s probably splinters all up inside that turkey. I know you’re all hungry but I can’t recommend eating this bird.
meanings.hint: You can’t recommend this turkey. It’s probably dangerous. Imagine sending this turkey back in a restaurant, very upset with everything. Also keep in mind that the arrow is half buried in the turkey and looks a little broken since the arrow radical is a bit off.

readings.mnemonic: You recommend the Genghis Khan ( かん ) special instead of this arrow-tainted turkey! When it comes out, your heart sinks. Genghis Khan apparently ate a cow with an arrow broken off in it.
readings.hint: Welp, I guess if Genghis Khan recommends eating food with weapons still in them, you're okay with recommending it too. Eat up, everyone.

meanings.mnemonic: To the servant you say: " gun , ground , plate ." It is your job to oversee everything they do.
meanings.hint: You point to the gun. You point to the ground (that's where the gun goes). You point to the plate (you want it). You have to oversee everything the servant does.

readings.mnemonic: Why must you oversee everything? Because you are Genghis Khan ( かん ), and Genghis Khan oversees everything.
readings.hint: If anyone had to oversee a lot, it was Genghis Khan, who had one of the largest kingdoms in all of history.

meanings.mnemonic: Under this roof you jackhammer your religion into people.
meanings.hint: It's a religion building. They have a lot of different names, churches, mosques, temples, cathedrals, but none of those matter. All that matters is that you really drill whichever religion you want into the heads of the people who come under your roof.

readings.mnemonic: Your religion requires everyone to take off their shoe s ( しゅう ). Mostly because the shoes will help them resist the jackhammering, so you tell them that to join your religion they have to leave their shoes at the door.
readings.hint: Picture the pile of shoes left outside. If you see a bunch of shoes like this you can be sure there's a religious place around here.

meanings.mnemonic: On every boat there is a lid on the table that's used for navigation . When you take the lid off, there is a big map, a sextant, and some little boat miniatures. All the navigation tools.
meanings.hint: You forget to put the lid back on the navigation station and all those tools and boat pieces go flying around the boat and into the water. Oops.

readings.mnemonic: You watch the navigation equipment roll away and then you feel a slap on the back of your head. Ouch! You turn around and there stands こう いち , who you took with you as the navigator of this expedition. And, you just lost all his stuff!
readings.hint: Picture this seafaring こういち angrily chasing you around the ruined navigation station, trying to hit you with a telescope.

meanings.mnemonic: If what you say comes out blue , everyone knows that means you made a request . You request a favor and the words slide out of your mouth in a bright, blue blob. They hover in front of you for a few seconds, before splashing onto the person you made the request to.
meanings.hint: Picture this blue, slimy request. You could have done that thing by yourself, but you requested it of someone else, and now it's all over and everyone can see it.

readings.mnemonic: The next time someone request s something from you, take out your sa ber ( せい ) and slice it in half!
readings.hint: Now they have the blue request gunk on them, and you can walk away as your already-blue sword absorbs the rest. Oh, and you don't have to fulfill the request (bonus!).

meanings.mnemonic: There is a cross in your forehead with a thread attached to it, forcing you to search where it leads. You’re not sure how the cross got stuck in your forehead, but you are interested in seeing where this piece of thread leads. The search is on.
meanings.hint: It hurts to have this cross stuck in your forehead, but try to ignore that as you search for where the thread leads. Imagine gathering the thread in your hands as you move along.

readings.mnemonic: Your search is over. You found the end of the thread. The thread leads you to a sack ( さく ). A sack filled with more crosses with more threads attached to them. What does it all mean!?!
readings.hint: Why are there more crosses and threads in the sack? Are you supposed to take them from the sack and throw them at other people’s foreheads? Maybe. Imagine yourself reaching into the sack and throwing the crosses at other people.

meanings.mnemonic: The soul of a narwhal was sucked into its towel , which is scary !
meanings.hint: Watch as the narwhal's face contorts as you see its soul get slurped out by the towel it was using.

readings.mnemonic: As you watch this scary scene you can't help but mutter, " Foo l," ( ふ ) under your breath. Then you hear "Fool, fool, fool, it's too scary you fool!" and you realize all of the other narwhals are chanting.
readings.hint: You don't know what's more scary. That the narwhal's soul got sucked out, or that all of its kind is calling it a fool now that it's dead.

meanings.mnemonic: The servant radical and the servant kanji are exactly the same! Just remember that the kanji also means retainer and you’ll be fine.
meanings.hint: Retainer and servant are basically the same thing, so remembering this one shouldn’t be too hard for you.

readings.mnemonic: Your servant , also known as your retainer , bangs his shins ( しん ) all the time so you make him wear jean s ( じん ) to work now. He bangs his shins on all the furniture in the house, knocking over all your valuables. But at least when he’s wearing jeans it doesn’t leave bruises like it did when he wasn’t wearing pants.
readings.hint: Imagine your servant banging his shins on everything in your house. Hand him a pair of jeans and tell him to put them on to soften the blows.

meanings.mnemonic: The leader of the mountain turkey s is our sponsor . He's paying us to say and type everything you're reading.
meanings.hint: This message is brought to you by our generous sponsor, the leader of the mountain turkeys. Long may he reign.

readings.mnemonic: This same sponsor is also how you made your cy borg ( さい ). You didn't think you were able to get all those body parts yourself, did you? No no, the leader of the mountain turkeys sponsored you so you could do your experiments.
readings.hint: Turn your cyborg over and you'll see a stamp under one of its feet. It says, "Sponsored by" with a little picture of a turkey on top a mountain.

meanings.mnemonic: If dirt and more dirt is all you see as you go from place to place, you must be roaming the streets of a city . Cities are notoriously dirty places, so watch your step when you head out into the streets.
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself caked in dirt as you make your way through this urban jungle — someone really needs to clean up these streets!

readings.mnemonic: As you roam the streets , brushing the dirt off yourself, a guy ( がい ) bumps into you: "Hey guy, watch where you're going!" As he says this he bumps into another guy. "Whoa there, guy — you think you own these streets or somethin'?" "As a matter of fact, my guy, I run these streets, so you'd better check yourself."
readings.hint: These two guys seem like they might be wise guys, so you hightail it out of there. You don't want to be another victim to the streets.

meanings.mnemonic: Say good luck to one person enough and they'll be stuffed full of that luck.
meanings.hint: Better safe than sorry. Don't just say good luck once, make sure they're stuffed!

readings.mnemonic: You stuffed them by saying good luck two ( つ ) whole times!
readings.hint: Picture a person with the actual words "good luck" stuffed into their body.

meanings.mnemonic: The servant sits on a stool , holding a piece of thread , looking very tense .
meanings.hint: You walk by this tense servant and pause for a moment. They raise their tense eyes up at you and you can see they're shaking.

readings.mnemonic: You ask the servant why they're so tense , and they say they've been assigned to assassinate the Kin g ( きん ) of England with this piece of thread.
readings.hint: Imagine how tense you would be if you were about to try to murder the King! Your entire body would tense up, maybe to the point that you wouldn't be able to actually go through with it.

meanings.mnemonic: At the gate , you kiss the cabinet members, one by one. In order to gain access to this government building, you have to kiss all the members of the cabinet at the gate. What a strange ritual.
meanings.hint: Imagine kissing all the members of the cabinet. A lot of them are old men. Imagine kissing all of them at the gate. Eww.

readings.mnemonic: You need to kiss each member of the cabinet to gain access to their building. And you need to get into the building to recover your cock ( かく ). Your prize winning cock has been captured by a member of the cabinet and you need to infiltrate their headquarters and recover your rooster!
readings.hint: Imagine yourself sneaking past the cabinet members, finding your cock, grabbing it, and getting out of there. You’ve recovered your rooster! The day is won.

meanings.mnemonic: When a yakuza 's knife boil s, you know something very violent and intense is about to happen.
meanings.hint: The yakuza member is so upset his knife boils. Right after, they will definitely get violent and intense.

readings.mnemonic: When someone gets violent or intense , they always yell " Let's ( れつ ) get violent and intense!"
readings.hint: In the case of our mnemonic, it's a yakuza member getting violent. So, imagine a yakuza member saying "Ret's get violent!" before doing just that.

meanings.mnemonic: You fly north to the moon and break your back when you finally land on it. You weren’t braced for impact so when you hit the moon, your back took all of the impact.
meanings.hint: Imagine flying north to the moon. Feel the force of being rocketed through space on your journey. Now feel your spine telescope and your back break. It really hurts.

readings.mnemonic:
Your back hurts so much that the only way to properly explain the pain is in hai ku ( はい ) form.

I flew to the moon. It is there I broke my back. My back hurts real bad.
readings.hint: Say this haiku out loud. Memorize it and use this haiku to remember this kanji.

meanings.mnemonic: Your leader sees your bare foot and urge s you to put a sock on it.
meanings.hint: Imagine your leader's face when he sees your bare foot, and just how emphatic he is as he urges you to put a sock on it. He's really worried your foot's going to get cold!

readings.mnemonic: As urge d, you put a sock ( そく ) on your foot. Ahh, that feels so much better. What a great thing, this sock.
readings.hint: Feel this sock on your foot. It feels great. Wiggle your toes around inside the sock as you think about how glad you are that you did as your leader urged. Note that this kanji also has the same reading as 足 , so keep that in mind too!

meanings.mnemonic: When you eat you want to see the shape of your gun from under a towel , so you decorate your house with them.
meanings.hint: Just picture it: an entire home decorated top to bottom with guns covered in towels, so only the outline shows. As your guests try to eat you say, "Guess what gun this is?! Haha, you got it wrong, pull the towel back."

readings.mnemonic: If you actually decorate your house like this it will give people quite a shock ( しょく ). In fact, they might be so shocked they run out of there screaming.
readings.hint: Maybe you should be more conscious of other people when you decorate. Or at least, don't shock people into fainting or yelling or leaving. Guns do that, you know?

meanings.mnemonic: A horse gets trapped in a small room with a stool and an insect , and together they create a very boisterous situation. The horse and the insect are both scrambling frantically to get out of the small room, knocking the stool around all over the place.
meanings.hint: Imagine the boisterous sounds coming from this small room. Hear the horse neigh and stomp his hooves, hear the insect buzzing loudly, and hear the wooden stool rattle around as the horse and insect try to escape the room.

readings.mnemonic: At the height of their boisterous sounds, there's a sudden, awful CRONCH(!) and you see the sou l ( そう ) of the insect slowly rising out of the room, through the ceiling.
readings.hint: The horse gets even more frightened now that it killed the insect and saw an actual soul float by its head and it grows even more boisterous.

meanings.mnemonic: A demon on a jet is pretty alluring . Demons themselves are pretty intriguing, but put one on a jet? Now that’s alluring.
meanings.hint: See this demon flying around in his jet. Do you feel allured? You should. Hear the roar of the jet engine as the demon cackles.

readings.mnemonic: This alluring demon on a jet wants one thing and one thing only. Me at ( み ). Human meat. This demon needs human meat to survive so he is taking his jet into the city to feast on some meat.
readings.hint: The demon won’t be so alluring once you see him scarfing down humans, tearing the meat from their bones. How gross.

meanings.mnemonic: Beforehand , to buy a geoduck , you have to go to the bank to deposit enough money. If you don't deposit your money you won't have enough in your account to buy one! They're expensive, after all.
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself depositing your money beforehand. Imagine the delicious geoduck you'll be able to buy now that the money has been deposited into your account.

readings.mnemonic: You go to the bank to make the deposit , and there is a kid standing in your way playing with a yo -yo ( よ ). You try to squeeze past and he hits you in the face with the yo-yo. Now you can't even see, let alone make the deposit.
readings.hint: Imagine trying to make a deposit to your bank after getting hit in the face with a yo-yo. You can hardly see what you're shoving your money into. Just hope it isn't the hands of the kid that hit you.

meanings.mnemonic: A tsunami hits the sun , where you're in prison with a spoon trying to catch some of it before it starts to dry up . Being in prison on the sun means your thirst is truly great, and this spoon will help you quench it when the tsunami comes.
meanings.hint: Imagine the feeling of drinking spoon after spoon of refreshing tsunami water, how it replenishes your dried up body. You've been in this sun prison for so long, holding onto this spoon and waiting for this moment. Better take advantage of it — even a tsunami will dry up real quick on the sun.

readings.mnemonic: This method of quenching your thirst reminds you of being in a ca r wa sh ( かわ ). The water pours over you in waves, and you're stuck inside a metal cage. Totally the same thing as a car wash, right?
readings.hint: Imagine quenching your thirst in a real car wash. Open your mouth and let the soapy water dump on over and in you. You'll never be thirsty again!

meanings.mnemonic: A tsunami knocks a cleat off a child and the shoe begins to float away in the water. This child was just out playing soccer, minding his own business, when the tsunami crashed in and knocked his cleats off his feet. Now the cleat is floating away with the tsunami.
meanings.hint: Your cleat is floating away but you’re floating too. The water is really deep now, but you try and float towards your cleat to retrieve it. Imagine floating in this wet water reaching for your lost cleat.

readings.mnemonic: While you float in the water, an u ni ( う ) jabs into your foot. “ Uwa aa~ ( うわ )” you cry. That uni really hurts. They’re all spikey and painful and junk. “Uwaaaa~”
readings.hint: Say “Uwaaa~” out loud a few times while you imagine an うに stabbing you as you float along with the tsunami water. What a day you’ve had.

meanings.mnemonic: If you're a one-sided paper anti (someone against one-sided paper), then you should bind all those one-sided papers into an edition .
meanings.hint: One-sided paper is just anti-everything. It's bad for the environment. It's bad for easy reading. Put all those one-sided papers together into one, big edition instead.

readings.mnemonic: The first edition you create is a fan fiction all about the adventures of Han ( はん ) Solo. You hated how short and one-sided his story in Star Wars seemed to be, and trying to keep track of it all was really hard. But not anymore!
readings.hint: Please read my super special Han Solo edition of the Star Wars story. I ripped out all the one-sided pages that had him in it and put them into a single edition together so it's just the good stuff now.

meanings.mnemonic: Traveling in the direction of the ocean, you take a gun and shoot a crab , then stick a flag in the beach and claim it as your own. The crab was the only thing standing in the way of you conquering the area. Now that the flag is in the ground, no one can dispute who it belongs to.
meanings.hint: Look at your flag and hear it flapping in the wind. Also hear your ears ringing a bit from when you shot that crab. Look at all the bits of exploded crab strewn around the area. Yowza.

readings.mnemonic: To make sure that no one removes your flag , you lock it to one of the lifeguard posts and you throw away the key ( き ). Since no one will have the key to unlock the flag, no one will be able to remove it.
readings.hint: Picture yourself locking this flag up with your key. Imagine turning the key in the lock and hearing the click when it is secured.

meanings.mnemonic: You've made yourself a tree bookshelf (that is, a bookshelf cut into a tree), so you build a fence to keep the riffraff out.
meanings.hint: Your tree bookshelf is often being raided by the neighbors, and since it's outside, all you can do is build a fence around it to protect it. Better check out that fence-building book in your tree bookshelf.

readings.mnemonic: On your fence , you hang up a sack ( さく ) to accept donations.
readings.hint: People put money and books in your fence sack to keep your tree bookshelf well stocked.

meanings.mnemonic: You run every day and soon you become a human that can go beyond the normal speed limits of man. You run and you run and you run. This training allows you to become super human. You go beyond the limits of a normal human. You become a human who can run really fast.
meanings.hint: Run fast! Go beyond the limits imposed upon you by nature! Imagine yourself running faster than any human has ever run ever. Feel yourself cutting through the air, wind breaking across you as you break the sound barrier. Boom!

readings.mnemonic: To solidify your ability to go beyond the abilities of other humans, you etch ( えつ ) sacred runes into your legs to preserve your running abilities. Etching these runes makes sure that your skills will never deteriorate.
readings.hint: Etch these runes into your legs. Go ahead and run a finger over your legs and pretend to etch runes into them. These runes you etch will allow you to run fast forever.

meanings.mnemonic: The sun is being call ed on to boil the earth by illuminating it.
meanings.hint: We don't want the earth to actually boil, but we do want some of that boiling hot sun light to illuminate all of the darkness of the universe and let us see.

readings.mnemonic: The person who calls the sun asking it to illuminate the earth is the Shou gun ( しょう ). It's very specifically the Shougun's responsibility to make sure the earth is illuminated every morning.
readings.hint: Picture the Shougun calling out to the sun, asking it to illuminate our planet with its boiling hot rays. You better thank the Shougun the next time you see him for everything he does for you.

meanings.mnemonic: A tsunami rips a flag off your house in the rain , causing a leak where it was ripped out. The rain and the tsunami combined just tore the flag right off your house. The force was so strong that it actually removed part of the wall, and now you have a leak in your home.
meanings.hint: Look at this leak in your house and see the rain water coming in. Hear it drip drip dripping down the wall.

readings.mnemonic: The leak is getting worse and worse and so is the tsunami, when you see something outside. It’s a bunch of roe ( ろう ) floating in the waves. Using your quick thinking skills you scoop it up and shove it into the cracks where the water is leaking in. It works like a charm!
readings.hint: Did you know that roe can plug any leak? It’s true. If you don’t mind wasting some, you should break a leak into your own house and then try to plug it back up again with some salmon roe.

meanings.mnemonic: The lineage radical and the lineage kanji are the same. Sometimes this kanji can also mean system . I guess a lineage is a kind of system, if you think about it.
meanings.hint: Basically, think of 系 as linking things that belong together in some kind of group. Lineages, solar systems, genres, styles… Anything along those lines .

readings.mnemonic: You trace your lineage back through your whole family system and realize every single relative loved ca ke ( けい ). Most of them were cake bakers too!
readings.hint: You come from a lineage of cake makers and eaters. No wonder you love cake so much! (And if you hate cake, no wonder your family doesn't like you.)

meanings.mnemonic: A servant holding a gun sits on the ground to see if he can find something to look at . The servant is on watch duty and has the gun to protect the perimeter. He patrols the grounds to look at the distance to see if there are any trespassers.
meanings.hint: Look at this servant. He’s doing a great job. Oops, looks like he sees you. Yup, he’s pointing his gun at you. Imagine staring down the barrel of his gun as you slowly back away.

readings.mnemonic: You start backing away, but then you realize that you are Macho Man Ran dy ( らん ) Savage. Randy Savage backs away from no fight! I mean, look at this guy – this servant guy. Take away the gun and he’s not much to look at. You can take him.
readings.hint: Pretend you are Macho Man Randy Savage. Run at this servant guy, take his gun away, and beat him up. Ohhh yeaaahhh!

meanings.mnemonic: You meet a woman from another clan under the rising sun to prepare for marriage . Your clan and her clan are standing together as the two of you watch the sun rise together.
meanings.hint: Marriage is a big deal and it's going to connect your two clans, so you have to do the ceremony in the sun in front of everyone so they can see.

readings.mnemonic: For the marriage to be official, you need to wait for a con dor ( こん ) to appear. If it doesn't show up before the sun goes down again, the marriage was a failure. You have to try again tomorrow with a different woman.
readings.hint: Do you want to go through with this marriage? You better hope you see a condor then! It's the official bird of love and unification, ya know. That's why so many people wait until they're older to get married these days, because there aren't as many condors thanks to deforestation.

meanings.mnemonic: Who is this woman mole ?! It's your niece !
meanings.hint: Who else would it be but your niece? In fact, all of your nieces are actually moles! Picture what your family gatherings would look like with all those mole-nieces. Quite terrifying, really…

readings.mnemonic: Not only are they moles, all of your niece s were also born in May ( めい )!
readings.hint: Picture your many nieces, all moles, and all born in the same month of May. Every year, like clockwork, a new mole-niece is born into your family in May.

meanings.mnemonic: Your soul will cross through your body and into the net of your clothes so that you'll be hit with nostalgia when you wear them again. Nostalgia for all the things that happened every single time you wore those clothes.
meanings.hint: When you put on an old piece of clothing, a shirt maybe, or some pants, feel the nostalgia that was embedded in it and left there by your soul.

readings.mnemonic: This nostalgia ties knots ( なつ ) in your stomach and your chest. Even if the memories and feelings weren't particularly bad, there's something about the wistful feeling of nostalgia that knots up your insides.
readings.hint: Feel these nostalgic knots inside you. Now tie knots in the clothes that make you feel nostalgic so you don't wear them again.

meanings.mnemonic: Point some fingers at the sun and put some others in your ear as you sit on a stool — now you can have your photograph taken. This is a glamour photo , so you have to be in one of these weird, artsy poses for it to come out juuust right.
meanings.hint: Which fingers do you point at the sun, and which do you put in your ear? Act it out, that'll help you remember. Now try to relax on the stool and smile for the camera. This is a professional photograph, so you better do what you're told!

readings.mnemonic: As you're getting the photograph taken, the photographer tells you to say Satsu ki ( さつ ). He says it's like saying cheese but more effective. Satsuki!
readings.hint: Go ahead and say Satsuki out loud, all happy-like. Makes a pretty big smile, right? From now on you should always say Satsuki when you're being photographed.

meanings.mnemonic: The soul of a person has a hook that can make them pleasant . When you see, hear, or feel something pleasant, can't you feel that hook pressing into the middle of your body?
meanings.hint: Imagine that feeling you get when everything is just SO pleasant. There is a pressure from your soul's hook digging in. It's a good feeling though, a pleasant one! It isn't too hard or too soft.

readings.mnemonic: If you want to feel this pleasant feeling just look at a kay ak ( かい ). Kayaks are the most pleasant boats in the world. Feel that pleasant feeling in your chest just from reading about kayaks in this mnemonic right now.
readings.hint: Go look up pictures of kayaks to get some of that pleasant high. Mm, your soul is hooking you real good.

meanings.mnemonic: The tree pirate jumps from a tree and attacks you by hitting you with a pillow .
meanings.hint: It hurts a little bit, but the fact that tree pirates use pillows as their weapons is a bit anticlimactic.

readings.mnemonic: As he hits you with his pillow he makes a demand: "Give me your Mac cooler ( まくら ). He wants the device that cools down your Mac computer.
readings.hint: You give him your Mac cooler and he goes on his way. He's quite happy he can cool down his Mac, now. The trees are quite warm and he hears this will help to cool down his tree apples.

meanings.mnemonic: Those that can only eat a part of a shellfish are probably a bit poor .
meanings.hint: Poor people can't even afford a whole shellfish. They have to settle with just a part.

readings.mnemonic: And since they are poor , they are getting these shellfish parts from the trash bin ( びん ). It's quite unfortunate.
readings.hint: Imagine a poor person going through a garbage bin to find only a part of of a shellfish. What a sad sight.

meanings.mnemonic: If you do yoga correct ly, you will not only prolong your body but your life!
meanings.hint: Do you want to prolong your life? Then do yoga correctly!

readings.mnemonic: You can prolong your life with correct yoga techniques, but unfortunately, if you ever stop doing yoga after you've started this prolonging process, it will just en d ( えん ) suddenly.
readings.hint: Correct yoga will prolong your life but no yoga will end it. Do you think that's a fair trade?

meanings.mnemonic: On a scooter running in one direction is a gun -toting child . This is a terribly dangerous situation, but to the child it is just play .
meanings.hint: Imagine a child riding a scooter with a gun having a good time. "Come play with me!" they shout. "No!" you yell back.

readings.mnemonic: Everything is play for you ths ( ゆう ), even if it's a terrible, scary, not good situation like this one.
readings.hint: Imagine trying to explain to the youth that this is not play and that they should stop, but them youths can't stop playing.

meanings.mnemonic: You give a shellfish to a lifeguard to purchase their services. You think lifeguards are interested in normal money? Come on, they're obviously out there using shellfish for their purchases. It's only natural, given their seaside habitat.
meanings.hint: Imagine the grooved surface of a shellfish shell as you press it into the hand of a lifeguard. Congrats, you've just completed a purchase. Uh… what'd you get?

readings.mnemonic: This time you purchase some こう いち goods. You know, since you're such a fan. How else can you show love in this world, if not through purchases? The lifeguard hands over a little haul of こういち plushies and figurines. Nice.
readings.hint: And it's totally because those こういち goods are so valuable that the lifeguard accepts shellfish for them. Has nothing to do with the fact he couldn't get real money for them, okay?

meanings.mnemonic: Take your fingers , put them on this turtle shell , and push push push! Push the turtle right on out of there.
meanings.hint: Picture yourself using your fingers to push a turtle out of its shell. It really doesn't want to leave, so you have to push really hard.

readings.mnemonic: You're trying to push this turtle out so you can use the shell for your new o bi ( お ). You keep seeing other people wearing turtle shell patterns on their obi, but you're going to go a step further. You're going to MAKE your obi out of actual turtle shells! You just have to push this darn turtle out first.
readings.hint: Imagine pushing some turtle shells flat to make a beautiful obi. Wear it out, watch everyone gasp in awe. Oh... oh no! They're trying to push you out of your obi like you did the turtle. They're mad about your turtle crime!

meanings.mnemonic: If you look for a leaf among the hills you will notice they are scarce . The hills have no trees around them, so there are no leaves. The leaves are scarce here on the hills.
meanings.hint: Imagine being on the hills. You want a leaf to find and take back as a souvenir but there are none. Imagine looking feverishly for a leaf even though you don’t really expect to find one.

readings.mnemonic: Still looking for a leaf, you determine that they are far too scarce to find here. Just before you give up, you discover a giant bow ling ( ぼう ) ball. As you pick it up, another one comes thundering down the hill knocking over a tiny tree.
readings.hint: Leaves are scarce here because someone is using these hills for their bowling. The bowling balls knock over everything, and leaves can't grow.

meanings.mnemonic: You're stuck in prison with nothing but a spoon , and the smell in here is so bad you’re using the prison spoon to waft it outside as best you can.
meanings.hint: It smells awful in this prison. You have to act fast with your prison spoon to get rid of all the bad smells!

readings.mnemonic: You can’t believe how awful these smell s are. You’re new in prison, so you call them all neo -smells ( にお ). They're neo, they're new to you.
readings.hint: Try to come up with some neo-smells right now. There’s the neo-food smell (prison cafeteria pizza, maybe) and the neo-laundry smell of course (your prison uniform). Can you think of any other neo-smells?

meanings.mnemonic: A scooter with a mohawk , now that's the most suitable hairstyle you could ever think of for a scooter!
meanings.hint: You keep seeing scooters all over the city with mohawks and you can't get it out of your head that it's the most suitable thing you've ever seen.

readings.mnemonic: You want this suitable hairstyle for your scooter too, and luckily you're a techie ( てき ), so it's easy for you to install one.
readings.hint: Imagine going online to check out some of your techie message boards and learning the most suitable mohawk for your scooter then installing it yourself.

meanings.mnemonic: The next plate is the one you will steal . This one would be too obvious, better wait for the next one.
meanings.hint: Wait for this next plate. Here it is—now steal it! Imagine stealing this plate and making off with it.

readings.mnemonic: You steal this plate from a とう きょう restaurant, so after you steal it, picture yourself running through the streets of とうきょう as you lose your pursuers.
readings.hint: Be careful not to drop that plate! Wouldn’t want to steal something just to break it. Imagine running through the streets of とうきょう . See all the sights and hear all the sounds. Just don’t get too distracted, you gotta get to a safe place and stash your stolen plate away.

meanings.mnemonic: Whenever all the feathers start stand ing, you know it's time for the next day. Once the day comes to its end, all feathers start standing up like clockwork.
meanings.hint: Imagine every single feather simultaneously standing up once the next day gets closer. It's like some kind of ritual that's repeated at the end of every single day. Must be stressful to be a feathered animal!

readings.mnemonic: With the next day approaching and all feathers standing up, you get ready to drink your usual liter of yolk ( よく ). Yes, you heard that right.
readings.hint: Imagine having to drink an entire liter of egg yolk at the end of each day. Just like the feathers have their ritual of standing up, you have your own ritual of downing a liter of egg yolk. Just don't ask questions.

meanings.mnemonic: You take some bamboo and attach it together into a token of appreciation for your friend. Your friend greatly appreciates this token of friendship.
meanings.hint: Imagine trying to attach some bamboo together into a token for someone.

readings.mnemonic: You hand over the token but your friend drops it and yells, "You foo l ( ふ )! I'm allergic to bamboo!"
readings.hint: Imagine the embarrassment you feel, forgetting they were allergic to bamboo. Oh well, it’s the thought that counts. You’re still a fool, though.

meanings.mnemonic: If a tsunami hits, it's rain ing, and you're outside trying to use a rake , you're going to be very wet and damp .
meanings.hint: Imagine how wet and damp you'd be with tsunami water up to your knees and rain pouring down on your head. You struggle with your rake anyway.

readings.mnemonic: You can't stand how wet and damp you feel in these clothes so you rip them off and rake in the nu de ( ぬ )!
readings.hint: You're still wet, but at least you don't have the feeling of damp clothes touching your body now. You'll have to be really careful raking in the nude though.

meanings.mnemonic: There's a mountain coming out of the ground that you want to climb, but your forehead needs a towel for all your sweat. You don't have a towel, so you take off your belt and tie it around your forehead.
meanings.hint: The belt is easy to tighten and it catches all the sweat before it can fall into your eyes. It isn't quite as absorbent as a towel though.

readings.mnemonic: The belt isn't absorbent enough, so you wrap it in your tie ( たい ) and then tie the whole thing around your forehead again.
readings.hint: It works! Your belt tied in a tie is the perfect invention!

meanings.mnemonic: The canopy guy is a guy who stands in the corridor trying to sell people canopies.
meanings.hint: You walk through this corridor every day, and the canopy guy always accosts you with canopy sales. You wish there was another corridor you could take, but it's the only way to get where you want to go.

readings.mnemonic: You take the corridor , but this time the canopy guy is gone. In his place is a new salesman. This one is trying to sell you roe ( ろう ).
readings.hint: The roe salesman in the corridor is worse than the canopy salesman. They're both insistent, but the roe salesman smells bad.

meanings.mnemonic: The rocket turkey has several points which will detach during takeoff.
meanings.hint: Imagine the rocket turkey taking off. Just like a regular rocket, as it goes up, used pieces of the turkey rocket detach and fall back to the ground.

readings.mnemonic: When the rocket turkey parts detach , they fall to the ground onto a bed of ree ds ( り ).
readings.hint: The reeds catch on fire - probably best not to fire off turkey rockets so close to these fields of reeds. Imagine the launch site completely surrounded by reeds.

meanings.mnemonic: You loiter around a tombstone to measure its diameter . You’re doing this to each tombstone, and if you loiter around long enough, you’ll soon know the diameter of every tombstone.
meanings.hint: Imagine loitering around a tombstone. You're leaning on it. You're staring at nothing. Oh, and you're also measuring its diameter.

readings.mnemonic: You need to know the diameter of each tombstone so you can make ca kes ( けい ) for each one. But the cakes can't be wider than the tombstones, or they'll flop off onto the ground.
readings.hint: These carefully measured tombstone-diameter cakes are your special way of saying thanks to the ghosts for letting you loiter around their graves.

meanings.mnemonic: You put a lid on a dish you're cooking, made up of the mouth s and forehead s of a pig . This is truly an exceptional dish you are cooking up.
meanings.hint: Mix those pig mouths and foreheads in your pot. Think of how exceptional your soup looks.

readings.mnemonic: Exceptional pig mouth forehead soup is a favorite of Grandpa ごう いち . You see him sniffing around your window, then he knocks and waves.
readings.hint: "Can I have some of your exceptional soup?" Grandpa ごういち asks. "It smells so swell."

meanings.mnemonic: The building s are at a surplus . Because of that, we will need to exclude some of them from our future plans. Also, we'll need to remove some buildings too.
meanings.hint: Say you're looking at a map of the city, with all the buildings laid out on it. There are too many, so you start marking which ones to exclude from the building plans. You also remove some buildings from the map all together.

readings.mnemonic: Working with you on this project to exclude buildings from the plan, as well as remove buildings that already exist is little Jo -Anne ( じょ ).
readings.hint: She's pointing out buildings to exclude / remove with great ferocity. "Remove this one. Exclude this one. Oh, and remove this one too."

meanings.mnemonic: You have some dirt next to a pool that has an extra drop of water in it because you need them to be equal . If there's too much dirt outside the pool and not enough water inside, it's gonna look weird. We can't have that.
meanings.hint: Imagine seeing a pool that isn't equal. It's like a tiny kiddy pool with an inch of water. And it's surrounded by mounds of dirt. No no no, this won't do—add more water to make them equal.

readings.mnemonic: You spend a few hours making sure the water-to-dirt ratio is perfectly equal , not for yourself, but because the Kin g ( きん ) of England told you to. You may agree that equality is important, but it's the King's responsibility to keep things that way. Which is why he ordered you, small peon, to do the equalizing work for him.
readings.hint: Okay, so the existence of a king kind of makes things not, well, equal, for all people. But at least aesthetically everything will be!

meanings.mnemonic: You see a bully take an insect and dissolve it in some acid. Bullies are messed up, mean people. They do awful things that normal kids and people wouldn't do... like dissolve a living creature.
meanings.hint: Actually... try to forget this one. Don't think about a bully dissolving things in acid. Think happy thoughts. Like... the bully getting dissolved instead and all the little insects coming back to life and being happy.

readings.mnemonic: The only bullies heartless enough to dissolve insects like this are you ths ( ゆう ). Do you really think an adult would melt a harmless critter? No. Only inexperienced youths could be so cruel.
readings.hint: When you first read this and you pictured the bully dissolving the insect, you pictured them as a youth, right? See? It makes complete sense.

meanings.mnemonic: A child shares his lineage with a grandchild . The child passes his lineage down to his child and then that child’s child is the grandchild and between them all a lineage is shared.
meanings.hint: Look at this family tree. See the child, and then the grandchild. Trace the lineage with your finger as you observe.

readings.mnemonic: The son ( そん ) of a son is a grandchild . In this case, a grandson. Easy.
readings.hint: Just remember that a son is all you really want. You need a son to carry on the family name and the lineage. So make sure that your child and your grandchild are both sons.

meanings.mnemonic: You want to change your shellfish into freight so you can transport it. That means packing them up into giant freight containers.
meanings.hint: Imagine changing your shellfish from loose, wet form, to solid freight form. It's actually a lot of hard work.

readings.mnemonic: To move this freight , you need a ca r ( か ). You strap all of the freight containers to the top of your car, stacked nice and high and precarious, so you can move it.
readings.hint: Picture all of this freight stacked on top of your car. As you drive the car, every turn you make feels slow and heavy from the freight weight.

meanings.mnemonic: Inside your greenhouse there's a mound of dirt which appears to be someone's grave . When you dig into it, you discover that it's an entire tomb , with doors and passages and everything!
meanings.hint: Why would someone put a grave in a greenhouse? Is this one of those "green burials" everyone's talking about these days? Having the whole tomb complex seems a tad counterproductive, though…

readings.mnemonic: You pry open the grave inside the inner chamber of the tomb , and are shocked to find the mummified body of Bo bo ( ぼ ) the Clown staring back at you! How terrifying!
readings.hint: You watch in horror as Bobo raises his arms and slowly rises from his grave. "At last, I have awoken from my slumber… Prepare yourselves, mortals — Bobo's taking reservations for birthday parties again!"

meanings.mnemonic: You poop twice on a sleeping drunkard person and you find yourself wondering how many more times you can poop on them and wonder how much they can take before they realize what’s going on.
meanings.hint: You’ve already pooped twice on this drunkard, how many more times can you do it? How much do you think this person can take? Just imagine yourself doing this.

readings.mnemonic: As you continue thinking about how much poop this drunkard can take and how many more poops you can drop on him, he begins to wake up. Quickly, you eke ( いく ) out one last poop before you scurry away. You eke that poop out just in time to make your escape.
readings.hint: Imagine yourself as you eke out that last poop. Really concentrate on eking it out so you can get away in time. Flex your butt muscles for added effect.

meanings.mnemonic: Wolverine is doing a construction job and using his mouth to take measurement s, so he knows how many folks he needs to inquire with about helping him. Using the mouth is a strange way to take measurements, but that's just how Wolverine does things. Once he measures it all out, he'll know how many construction workers he needs to inquire with about working for him.
meanings.hint: Imagine Wolverine going around, measuring the construction site with his mouth. Look at him with his mouth wide open, wandering around the site deep in thought. What a weirdo.

readings.mnemonic: Another thing Wolverine has to inquire about is jean s ( じん ). He wants to make sure everyone on the construction site has good-fitting jeans to work in, so he inquires with them about their jean measurements.
readings.hint: Imagine Wolverine handing you some new jeans at the construction site. Um, they're kind of all ripped up? Turns out that's just the style he likes. You'll be so fashionable now! Just like good old '90s Wolvie.

meanings.mnemonic: Bring thread to the library to knit . Libraries are quiet and generally pretty empty nowadays, so it's the perfect place to relax and do some knitting. Of course, if you find thread lying around in a library you know what to do now.
meanings.hint: Picture yourself sitting on a rocking chair in the library knitting away. It's quiet and peaceful and the light is warming you just right. Ahh, bliss!

readings.mnemonic: You need to come to the library to knit because if you try to do it at home all your hen s ( へん ) will try to eat the thread. Hens love eating things that look like worms, like hay, string, and thread. So it's important to get far away from them before you start to knit.
readings.hint: Imagine the granny or grampy you are (and must be to have hens and want to knit in peace in a library). You're tired of these hens eating your thread while you try to knit sweaters for all your grandbabies so you come to the library.

meanings.mnemonic: Put your fingers to your forehead and stand your legs high up in a tree if you want to look for something. After all, shielding your eyes and getting up high is the best way to look for something, isn’t it?
meanings.hint: Imagine it's like a quest from a book or game. What are you looking for? Maybe a powerful sword, or a lost princess. Picture it in your mind, this thing you're looking for.

readings.mnemonic: You look for it for so long that you can't help getting a tan ( たん ). I mean, you're up some tree, shielding your eyes from the sun as you look for that thing, and it's been hours, days? Of course you're going to get a tan.
readings.hint: Feel the sun's rays on your skin as you search and search and search. Watch as your skin gets darker, crispier, and — hmm — starts smelling like cooked meat. Now that's a good tan.

meanings.mnemonic: A building made from a womb must be placed upon the land .
meanings.hint: Why must it be placed upon the land? If it were put in water, it would dissolve too quickly. So, you put your womb building on the land, instead.

readings.mnemonic: The land around your womb building will, unfortunately, reek ( りく ) though.
readings.hint: It's made of biological materials, so of course it will reek.

meanings.mnemonic: See that gold man oversee ing everyone? He's a model human. His goldenness is a sign of how exemplary he is, and he's overseeing all of us flawed, regular folks to try to help us get on his level.
meanings.hint: Imagine this golden man standing on a raised platform, looking down at you judgmentally with his arms crossed. You mutter curses at him under your breath, but really you're just jealous you're not a model human like him.

readings.mnemonic: As you look closer at the golden model human, you realize he's actually Genghis Khan ( かん )! No wonder he's covered in gold and overseeing everyone — he's the greatest conqueror in history!
readings.hint: Hold on a sec — didn't Genghis Khan kill a bunch of people and father like a thousand kids? He doesn't seem like such a model person to you, but, hey, you don't make the rules around here — Genghis Khan does.

meanings.mnemonic: A tsunami came in and buried your flag so now you have to use a spoon and dig it out of the mud .
meanings.hint: Imagine using a spoon to dig your flag outta all this mud. So much mud! Feel its cold, soiled wetness on your hands as you dig away.

readings.mnemonic: You may know of Dora the Explorer. But have you heard about her dirty, mud dy brother, Doro ( どろ )? They're like a team. And because of all the exploring he does, Doro is always covered in mud.
readings.hint: Imagine them working together — Dora and Doro, the Muddy Explorers! Who knows why she's able to stay clean, while Doro gets so muddy…

meanings.mnemonic: Birds use grass and fruit to make their nest s.
meanings.hint: Picture a cute little nest with a family of birds in it. It looks like it was woven together with bits of grass and strawberries, so it smells delicious too.

readings.mnemonic: The bottom of the nest also seems to be padded with someone's su it ( す ). Did... did this bird attack a human for this?!
readings.hint: Picture the nest, made of grass, fruit, and the suit of the angry salaryman who's shouting below the tree, and missing the top half of his suit.

meanings.mnemonic: You line up your body with the sun and everything looks normal .  The sun is lined up behind you, and you’re looking at all the things the sun has lit up. Everything looks normal.
meanings.hint: Gaze out in a line from the sun as you observe the world. Everything is very normal looking and comforting.

readings.mnemonic: Soon you see something that doesn’t look so normal . It’s a foo l ( ふ ), a jester. Dressed all in fool’s garb, this fool is dancing up towards you with a ridiculously unsettling grin on his face.
readings.hint: Imagine this creepy looking fool as he approaches. He’s kind of freaking you out. Feel very uncomfortable as you continue looking at this fool.

meanings.mnemonic: A woman with direction will have things obstruct her path. It's a sad fact that it's still a man's world out there!
meanings.hint: Picture this woman with direction, knocking down obstruction after obstruction on her path to success.

readings.mnemonic: This woman is truly magnificent. Some compare her to a bow ling ( ぼう ) ball, because of how she knocks down the obstructions in her path like bowling pins.
readings.hint: Now imagine that she actually is a bowling ball. Watch her roll down the bowling lane and smash all those stupid pins!

meanings.mnemonic: A tree in spring that's nice and dry will make a good pole or rod . Break it out of the ground and give it a swing to see what a nice, sturdy stick it is!
meanings.hint: Use this pole to hang your towels or curtains. Ooh, use it to pole vault!

readings.mnemonic: After much deliberation, you decide to use this pole to go bow ling ( ぼう ). It's a pretty long pole, so you can just stand at the top of the lane and directly poke the bowling pins with it.
readings.hint: Imagine all the irritated glares you get as you use your pole to cheat your way through several games of bowling. That's against the rules, buddy!

meanings.mnemonic: If you grind rice , every part of it, eventually it will become a fine powder . You can then use this rice powder as a part to create some other dish. Some kind of cake, perhaps.
meanings.hint: Imagine yourself grinding this rice into a powder. Hear the scrape of the rice on stone as you grind it down to the finest powder.

readings.mnemonic: Actually, grinding the rice down to a powder is super fun ( ふん ). It’s so fun you don’t want to stop! AH HAHAHAHA! RICE POWDER! SO FUN!
readings.hint: Okay, we got a little carried away there. Just keep in mind how fun this is. Make sure you say fun with a little bit of a Scottish accent. You know what they say, Scots have more fun!

meanings.mnemonic: There's a root where your fang previously was. Your fang isn't there anymore, you ripped it out.
meanings.hint: Look at the root where your fang previously was. Previously, when you were young, you had a lot of nice fangs but they're all rotting as you get older and you pull them out.

readings.mnemonic: To remember the beautiful fangs you previously had, you keep all of the ones you pull out in a box you lock with a key ( き ).
readings.hint: Previously you didn't have a key, but now you do to keep your old fangs safe.

meanings.mnemonic: You request that winter rescue you from the warmth of the other seasons.
meanings.hint: It's so hot. You hate it. You really hope winter will get your request and come early to rescue you.

readings.mnemonic: You get a note in the mail from winter! This is it, you're going to be rescued! You open the note and it says, "I have rescue d you with this cu cumber ( きゅう )." A cucumber falls out of the note.
readings.hint: This cucumber is supposed to be cool and refreshing enough to rescue you from the heat. I guess it was too much to expect winter to come early just for you. At least cucumbers are yummy.

meanings.mnemonic: A leader of (odd-looking) lion s puts a drop of magic liquid on his face to look like a person who resemble s someone else. The leader of lions is going incognito, so he needs to resemble a person who is not himself. He takes this drop of powerful face paint and transforms.
meanings.hint: Watch this leader as the transformation takes place. Now he doesn’t resemble anything like the leader he used to be. He doesn’t look like the same person. Watch him morph in your head.

readings.mnemonic:
Now he resemble s an even greater leader, and all the lions get down on one knee ( に ) to pay their respects.

(Note that we recently changed this kanji's main reading from ね to に , because ね is an irregular reading.)
readings.hint: See all the odd-looking lions respectfully getting down on one knee? They look even odder now.

meanings.mnemonic: On your roof is a scarecrow because you're rich . You want to scare away all the poor, non-rich people so you put a scarecrow on your roof.
meanings.hint: Imagine looking up at the roof at this scarecrow. There's a sign around its neck that says, "Rich people welcome." Ah, you're safe. Time to go to bed!

readings.mnemonic: You wake up the next morning and all of your rich es have been stolen! You call the police and they call you a foo l ( ふ )! Pardon me?! "You basically put an advertisement on your roof telling everyone you're rich. Of course you got robbed, you fool."
readings.hint: You poor sad, no longer rich fool.

meanings.mnemonic: If you play blackjack on the moon in the winter , your cards will scatter everywhere. The cards scatter because the gravity is so weak on the moon, so the blackjack game is prone to disaster.
meanings.hint: Imagine playing blackjack on the moon where even the slightest move causes the cards to scatter about. Watch them float along. It’s very frustrating to play this way.

readings.mnemonic: You are playing blackjack with San ta-san ( さん ). Santa-san is doing his best to use his Christmas magic to prevent the scatter of cards, but it’s not working out so well. Good effort, Santa-san.
readings.hint: Imagine yourself next to Santa-san as he tries to prevent the cards from scattering. Look at the expression of concentration on Santa-san’s face as he tries to prevent the scatter.

meanings.mnemonic: If you want to go to Korea you'll need a good defense .
meanings.hint: Things in Korea haven't been on the safest side lately, so make sure you have some ways to defend yourself before you go.

readings.mnemonic: You need a good defense to go to Korea, so you fill your pockets with a corns ( えい ). When you get to Korea you plant them all around yourself. In no time you'll have a strong tree defense, keeping you safe.
readings.hint: Okay, so maybe this defense strategy will take a while, but you aren't rich and acorns are on the ground for free. Don't judge!

meanings.mnemonic: A tree combines with a table to make a desk . When you think about it, a desk is just a table with more stuff. To make this more stuff, like drawers and such, you need more wood, and wood comes from trees. Add materials from the tree to the table and you have a desk.
meanings.hint: Chop this tree into pieces to transform this table into a desk. Hear the sounds of construction in your head as you make this desk with your bare hands.

readings.mnemonic:
When you finish the desk you turn to your friend and say, “ It’s cool, eh ( つくえ )?” Your friend looks at you and says, “S’coo’ eh? What?” “This desk, it’s cool. It’s cool, eh?” “Aw, yeah man. S’coo’ eh.”

Another way to remember this reading is to think about where you find lots of desks. That's right, in a school ( つくえ ).
readings.hint: Just imagine someone slurring “It’s cool, eh?” While you think about how cool this desk really is. Run your hands over the finished project as you say it.

meanings.mnemonic: A tsunami hit not one, but TWO prison s, making them extremely dirty .
meanings.hint: Imagine observing the two prisons being hit by a giant wave of muddy water, making them completely dirty. How disgusting!
